docs.title: BigCommerce Page Builder Widgets - PapaThemes Docs
docs.text: Welcome to the official documentation for PapaThemes BigCommerce Widgets.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: PapaThemes Widgets is a collection of custom widgets designed for BigCommerce Page Builder. These widgets help you easily add rich UI components to your website without writing code.

docs.title: Quick Links
docs.text:
• AI Widgets

    AI-powered content creation widgets

    AI HTML Widget

  • Basic Widgets

    Core widgets for common UI patterns

    Accordion Image Gallery CKEditor Responsive

  • Beautify Widgets

    Enhanced widgets for Beautify themes

    Flex Banners Products Feed Category Icons

  • Theme-Specific

    Widgets for specific PapaThemes themes

    Chiara Theme Dinosaur Theme Eyeva Theme

docs.title: Key Features
docs.text:
• ✅ Page Builder Compatible - Works seamlessly with BigCommerce's drag-and-drop interface
  • ✅ Flexible Customization - Multiple configuration options for each widget
  • ✅ Responsive Design - Displays beautifully on all devices
  • ✅ Inline Editing - Supports direct content editing on the page
  • ✅ Multi-Theme Support - Compatible with multiple PapaThemes themes

docs.title: Getting Started
docs.text:
New here? Follow the Getting Started guide to install the app and add your first widget. In short:

  1. Install the PapaThemes Widgets app from the BigCommerce App Marketplace
  2. Open the app and install the widgets you want
  3. Open Page Builder and find the widgets in the Custom section of the sidebar
  4. Drag and drop a widget onto your page
  5. Configure it in the settings panel, then Save and Publish

Read the full Getting Started guide

docs.title: Accordion Widget
docs.text:
The Accordion widget shows a list of expandable, collapsible sections. Shoppers click a heading to reveal or hide the content underneath. Use it for FAQs, product details, shipping and returns info, or anywhere you want to keep a page tidy.

Page Builder name: Accordion | PapaThemes — Version: 1.0.1

docs.title: Add it to your page
docs.text:
1. In Page Builder, open the widget panel and find Accordion | PapaThemes.
  2. Drag it onto the page where you want the accordion to appear.
  3. The widget starts with two sample items. Edit them in the settings panel and add more as needed.

docs.title: General
docs.text: Option What it does Default Border Color The line around the whole accordion and between items. #858585 Focus Color The highlight color shown when an item is selected or navigated to with the keyboard. #0759d5

docs.title: Items
docs.text:
Each accordion section is an entry in the Items list.

  • Add an item: open the Items list and choose to add a new entry.
  • Remove an item: use the delete (trash) control on the entry you want to remove.
  • Reorder items: drag entries up or down to change the order they appear on the page.

Options for each item:

Option What it does Default Open by default When on, this section starts open when the page loads. Off Title The heading text shoppers click. Type your text and format it with the visual editor. Accordion Title Body The content shown when the section is open. Type your text and format it with the visual editor. Accordion Body

Edit text right on the page

You can click a title or content area directly on the page in Edit Mode and type your changes there. Your edits are saved with the page.

docs.title: Tips
docs.text:
Keep it scannable

Use clear, short titles so shoppers can quickly find the section they need. Accordions work best when each section answers one question or covers one topic.

Match your brand

Leave Typography on Default to inherit your theme's font, or switch to Custom when a section needs to stand out.

  • Open your most important section by default so shoppers see key info without clicking.
  • Avoid very long content inside a single section; consider splitting it into multiple items.
  • Pick a heading Tag that fits the page outline (for example, H2 or H3 under a main page title) for better accessibility and SEO.

docs.title: What is the AI HTML Widget?
docs.text:
The AI HTML Widget is your personal design assistant inside BigCommerce Page Builder. Instead of hunting for the right widget or hiring a developer, you simply describe the section you want — a hero banner, a feature grid, a countdown timer, a signup form — and the AI builds it and places it on your page for you.

You chat with it in plain English. You can ask for changes as many times as you like, paste in a picture for inspiration, or start from one of hundreds of ready-made templates. It is the fastest way to add polished, on-brand content to your store without touching any code.

Page Builder name: AI HTML Generator | PapaThemes

Version: 1.3.1

When to use it

Reach for this widget any time the standard Page Builder widgets do not give you the exact look you want — custom layouts, promotional sections, interactive elements, landing pages, and more.

docs.title: What You Can Do
docs.text:
• Generate complete page sections just by describing them
  • Apply the AI's work to your page instantly, with one message
  • Edit any element visually by clicking it — a docked design panel opens, no chatting required
  • Duplicate, move, or delete any element with a floating toolbar
  • Arrange row, column, and grid sections with visual layout controls
  • Tune styles per breakpoint for Desktop, Tablet, and Mobile
  • Start from a gallery of ready-made section and popup templates
  • Paste an image as a reference, or ask the AI to create an image for you
  • Refine anything with simple follow-up requests
  • Step back to an earlier version if you change your mind
  • Stop a long answer at any time and keep going
  • Compact a long conversation to keep your credit usage low

docs.title: 1. Add the widget to your page
docs.text:
1. Open Page Builder in your BigCommerce admin.
  2. In the widget panel, find AI HTML Generator | PapaThemes.
  3. Drag and drop it onto the spot where you want your new content.
  4. The widget shows a friendly placeholder card until you generate something.

docs.title: 2. Open the AI assistant
docs.text:
1. After adding the widget, a purple AI button appears in the bottom-right corner of Page Builder.
  2. Click it to slide open the chat assistant from the right side of the screen.

Note

The chat assistant is only available while you are editing in Page Builder. On your live storefront, visitors simply see the finished content.

docs.title: 3. Describe what you want
docs.text:
1. Type a description of the section you want into the message box.
  2. Click Send (or press Enter).
  3. The AI builds the section and applies it to your page automatically as you watch.
  4. A new version is saved so you can always come back to it.

docs.title: 4. Refine it
docs.text: Send follow-up messages to fine-tune the result. The assistant remembers the whole conversation, so you can say things like "make the heading bigger" or "change the button to match my brand color" and it will adjust what it already built.

docs.title: The Welcome Screen and Starter Prompts
docs.text: The first time you open the assistant, a short welcome message introduces what it can do. Below it you will find starter prompt buttons — one-click ideas like building a hero section or a feature grid. They are a quick way to see the assistant in action if you are not sure where to begin. Just click one and watch it work, then customize from there.

docs.title: Writing Effective Prompts
docs.text:
The more clearly you describe what you want, the better the result. A few simple habits make a big difference.

Be specific about the content. Instead of "make a banner," try: "Create a full-width hero banner with a background image, a large white headline centered in the middle, a short subtitle, and a blue 'Shop Now' button."

Mention how it should look on mobile. For example: "Show three columns on desktop and stack them into one column on phones."

Reference your brand. Say "match my store's color scheme" — the assistant can look at your current page to pick up your fonts, colors, and spacing.

Build up step by step. Start with the overall layout, then refine colors, spacing, and wording in follow-up messages.

Show, don't just tell. Paste a screenshot of a design you like and say "recreate something like this."

Tip

Starting from a ready-made template (see below) and then describing your changes is often faster than describing an entire design from scratch.

docs.title: Ready-Made Templates
docs.text: Rather than starting with a blank page, you can browse a large library of professionally designed section and popup templates and drop one onto your page with a single click.

docs.title: Browse and apply a template
docs.text:
1. Open the AI assistant.
  2. Below the message box, click Browse Blocks to open the full template gallery.
  3. Browse by category, filter by tags, or search by name.
  4. Click any template to open a full-size preview with Desktop, Tablet, and Mobile views so you can see how it looks on every device.
  5. Click Use This Block to apply it to your widget. A new version is saved automatically.
  6. The assistant then invites you to customize it: "Tell me what to customize."

After applying a template, just tell the assistant what to change — colors, text, images, layout, timing, anything.

Browse the full template catalog

See the Section Templates Gallery for a visual catalog of the form and popup templates, with screenshots and descriptions.

docs.title: Paste an image for reference
docs.text:
1. Copy any image to your clipboard — a design mockup, a screenshot, or inspiration from another site.
  2. Click in the message box and press Ctrl+V (Cmd+V on Mac).
  3. A thumbnail appears above the box. Add a description and send.

Great uses include recreating a mockup, showing the assistant your current page, or pointing out something that looks off so it can fix it. You can attach more than one image to a single message, and remove any thumbnail before sending by clicking the X on it.

docs.title: Ask the AI to create an image
docs.text: You can also ask the assistant to generate an image for you right inside the conversation — for example, "add a background photo of a cozy coffee shop." The generated image is placed into your section automatically.

docs.title: Stop a long response
docs.text: If a response is taking longer than you need, you can stop it mid-way. Anything generated up to that point is kept, and you can carry on with your next request. If you type a new message while the AI is still working, it will be sent automatically as soon as the current response finishes.

docs.title: Compact the conversation to save credits
docs.text: Long conversations cost more, because the assistant re-reads the whole chat each time. The compact conversation button condenses the earlier back-and-forth into a short summary — keeping the important context while reducing how much you are charged on future messages. Use it when a chat has been going for a while.

docs.title: Go back to an earlier version
docs.text:
Every time the AI builds or updates your section, a new version is saved. Use the version arrows at the top of the chat (for example, v2/5) to step back and forth. The page updates instantly as you move between versions, so you never lose earlier work. If you step back and then send a new message, the assistant continues from that earlier version.

Tip

Start a fresh conversation (the Clear button) when you move on to a completely different design. This keeps each chat focused and your credit usage low.

docs.title: Visual Editor (Design Mode)
docs.text: Sometimes you want to nudge a colour, shrink a padding, or change a heading size without typing a full message to the AI. The Visual Editor lets you edit any element of your section directly, just like a page-builder design tool.

docs.title: Selecting an element
docs.text:
1. Just click any element in your section inside Page Builder. The right-side Design panel opens straight away with that element's current styles — you no longer need to turn on a separate Design Mode first.
  2. Links and buttons still behave normally when you click them, so your section keeps working as your shoppers would use it.
  3. If you click an element that has no styles of its own, the editor automatically selects its nearest styled parent — so you always have something useful to edit.

You can still switch between asking the AI and editing by hand using the Chat and Design buttons at the top of the chatbot sidebar. Your conversation is preserved while you design.

docs.title: Follow the element path
docs.text: At the top of the Design panel, a breadcrumb path shows where the selected element sits — listing the parent elements above it. Click any parent in the path to jump up and select it, which is the quick way to grab the whole row, card, or section rather than the small piece inside it. If the path is very long, it tucks the middle away behind a … that you can click to expand.

docs.title: Quick actions on the selected element
docs.text:
A small floating toolbar sits on the element you have selected, giving you one-click structural changes without chatting:

  • Duplicate — make an exact copy right next to it (handy for adding another card, column, or list item).
  • Move up / Move down — reorder the element among its neighbours.
  • Delete — remove the element from your section.

docs.title: What you can change
docs.text:
The Design panel groups styles into familiar sections:

Group Controls Text Edit the visible text directly inline. Typography Font family, size, weight, line height, letter spacing, alignment. Color Text color and background color (solid colors). Spacing Padding and margin on all four sides (box model). Border Border width, style, color, and radius.

Edits preview live as you adjust each control, and they are saved automatically as you go (see Automatic saving and Restore below).

docs.title: Layout controls for rows, columns, and grids
docs.text:
When the element you select is one that arranges its children — a row, a column, or a grid — a Layout section appears in the Design panel with visual, icon-based controls, much like a professional design tool. You can set:

  • Direction — lay the children out in a row or a column.
  • Wrapping — let the children flow onto a new line when they run out of room, or keep them on one line.
  • Gaps — the spacing between children, set separately for rows and columns.
  • Alignment — how the children line up and spread out along and across the layout.

Like the other controls, the Layout section can be adjusted separately for Desktop, Tablet, and Mobile, so you can, for example, show items side by side on desktop and stack them on phones.

docs.title: Responsive editing — Desktop, Tablet, Mobile
docs.text:
At the top of the panel, three buttons show the active breakpoint: Desktop, Tablet, Mobile. The active one follows your Page Builder preview width automatically, so what you see is what your shoppers will see at that size.

When you edit on Tablet or Mobile, your change is saved as a responsive override for that screen size only — Desktop stays untouched. This is the same approach professional design tools use.

docs.title: Shared classes — "affects N elements"
docs.text:
The AI builds sections using shared styles for repeated elements (think a row of feature cards or a grid of product tiles). If the element you are editing shares its style with others, you will see a small badge — for example "affects 3 elements".

That means your change will apply to all matching elements at once. This is usually what you want — keeps the design consistent. If you only want to change one of them, ask the AI in chat to give that element its own style.

docs.title: Automatic saving and Restore
docs.text:
Your design edits are now saved automatically as you make them — there are no longer any Save changes or Discard buttons to remember.

If you want to undo your hand-edits, click Restore. This button only appears once you have made a change in the Design panel, and it reverts all of your design-panel edits back to the original look of the current AI version. Anything the AI generated stays in place — only your manual tweaks are rolled back.

Tip

Use the Visual Editor for targeted tweaks — colors, spacing, font sizes, layout, and duplicating, reordering or deleting elements. For bigger creative changes ("add a button that opens a popup", "write me a new headline"), it is still faster to ask the AI in chat.

docs.title: Quality Levels and Credits
docs.text:
The assistant offers three quality levels, which you choose from the dropdown at the top of the chat. Your choice is remembered for next time.

Level Best for Mini Quick edits — text changes, color tweaks, small adjustments. Fastest and cheapest. Standard Most everyday work — new sections, forms, and interactive elements. A great balance of quality and cost. Max Complex, multi-part layouts where you want the very best quality. Powered by Anthropic's latest Claude Opus model.

docs.title: How credits work
docs.text:
The widget itself is free. The AI work is paid for with credits in your PapaThemes account (measured in US cents — a $10 top-up gives you 1,000 credits).

  • You are charged a small amount once per response, based on the quality level you chose and the length of the request and result.
  • A running cost and usage meter is shown at the bottom of the chat so you always know where you stand.
  • If your balance runs out, the assistant lets you know and you can top up at any time.

Save credits

  • Use Mini for small changes, Standard for most work, and Max only for complex layouts.
  • Use the compact conversation button on long chats, or start a fresh chat when switching tasks.
  • Be specific in your prompts to avoid extra back-and-forth.
  • Crop images to just the relevant area before pasting.

You can check your balance and buy more credits in the PapaThemes app in your BigCommerce admin.

docs.title: Saving Your Chat History (Optional)
docs.text:
By default, your conversation and saved versions live in your browser and are cleared when you close the tab. An optional history-saving subscription keeps everything safely on the server instead, so your conversations, images, and versions are restored every time you reopen the assistant — even on another device.

  • A free trial starts automatically the first time you use the assistant.
  • When the trial nears its end, you will see a notice with options to turn on auto-renew, switch back to browser-only, or dismiss the message.
  • With auto-renew on and enough credits, it renews automatically with no interruption.
  • You can manage the subscription, view your balance, and buy credits in the PapaThemes app under Settings.

Note

The subscription only affects whether your history is kept between sessions. All of the assistant's features — generating content, templates, images, quality levels — work the same with or without it.

docs.title: Page Builder Settings
docs.text:
Most of the time you control the widget entirely through the chat assistant, but two settings are available in the Page Builder panel when the widget is selected:

Setting What it does Default HTML Content Shows the current content of the widget. This fills in automatically as the AI works — you normally do not need to touch it. Welcome card Show in container div Centers the content and constrains it to your page's standard content width. Off

Tip

Turn on Show in container div if you want your section to line up neatly with the rest of your page content rather than stretching edge to edge.

docs.title: Example Prompts to Try
docs.text:
"Create a hero section with a large background image, a centered headline, a subtitle, and a call-to-action button. Add a dark overlay on the image so the text is easy to read."

"Build a 3-column grid showing product features. Each column has an icon, a title, and a description. Stack to one column on mobile."

"Create a countdown timer to December 25th showing days, hours, minutes, and seconds, with large white numbers on a dark background."

"Design a testimonials carousel with customer quotes, names, and star ratings. Show three at a time on desktop, one on mobile, rotating every five seconds."

"Build an FAQ section with five questions that expand and collapse when clicked."

"Create a newsletter signup with a heading, a short description, an email field, and a submit button on a gradient background."

docs.title: Tips for Best Results
docs.text:
• Be specific — the clearer your description, the closer the first result will be.
  • Mention mobile behavior so your section looks great on every screen.
  • Reference your brand so colors and fonts match your store.
  • Iterate in small steps rather than asking for everything at once.
  • Use images — paste a mockup and ask the assistant to recreate it.
  • Start from a template when one fits, then customize.
  • Compact or clear long conversations to keep credit usage low.

docs.title: Do I need any coding experience?
docs.text: No. You describe what you want in plain English and the assistant does the rest. You never see or edit code.

docs.title: How much does it cost?
docs.text: The widget is free. You only pay credits for the AI work, based on the quality level you pick and the size of the request. Use Mini for small jobs to keep costs down.

docs.title: Can I use it with any BigCommerce theme?
docs.text: Yes. It works with all BigCommerce themes, and the content it creates stays neatly contained so it does not interfere with the rest of your store's design.

docs.title: Why isn't the AI button showing?
docs.text: Make sure you are in Page Builder edit mode and that the widget is on the page. Refreshing Page Builder usually brings it back.

docs.title: Can I undo a change?
docs.text: Yes. Every change is saved as a version. Use the version arrows at the top of the chat to step back to any earlier version — nothing is lost.

docs.title: Will my section look good on phones and tablets?
docs.text: Yes. The content the assistant builds is responsive and adapts to desktops, tablets, and phones. You can also ask it to adjust the mobile layout specifically.

docs.title: Is my chat history saved?
docs.text: By default it lives in your browser for the current session. Turn on the optional history-saving subscription to keep your conversations, images, and versions across sessions and devices.

docs.title: Callout Icons Widget
docs.text:
Show a row of icons, each with an optional heading and short text, to highlight your store's key features or services — such as free shipping, fast support, secure checkout, or easy returns. You can choose from a large built-in icon set, upload your own icon image, or paste custom icon code.

Page Builder name: Callout Icons | PapaThemes Beautify — Version: 1.0.1

docs.title: Add it to your page
docs.text:
1. In your BigCommerce admin, open Storefront → My Themes and click Customize to launch Page Builder.
  2. In the left Widgets panel, find Callout Icons | PapaThemes Beautify.
  3. Drag it onto the page — a USP strip near the top of the homepage or above the footer works well.
  4. The widget appears with four sample callouts. Click it to open the settings panel and edit them.

docs.title: Settings
docs.text: All settings live inside the Callouts list — there is one entry per icon.

docs.title: Callouts
docs.text:
This is the repeatable list of callouts. Each entry is labeled Callout.

  • Add a callout with the Add button at the bottom of the list.
  • Remove a callout using its delete icon.
  • Drag callouts by their handle to reorder them.

Each callout has a Content tab with the options below. You type the heading and the second text directly on the callout in Page Builder.

docs.title: Tips
docs.text:
Use the same style for every callout

For a clean, balanced row, give every callout the same Style, Alignment, and icon size.

  • Keep the heading to a few words (such as "Free Shipping") and put the detail in the second text.
  • Add a Link to each callout so shoppers can tap through to the relevant page (shipping policy, returns, contact, and so on).
  • Use Choose Image or Custom HTML/SVG when you need a brand-specific icon that is not in the built-in set.
  • Turn on Hide on mobile for the second text to keep the strip compact on small screens.

docs.title: Category Icons Widget
docs.text:
Display a row of category tabs, each with its own icon. When a shopper clicks a tab, the products from that category load right below. You control which product details appear, the icons, colors, typography, and spacing.

Page Builder name: Category Icons | PapaThemes — Version: 1.0.3

docs.title: Add it to your page
docs.text:
1. In your BigCommerce admin, open Storefront → My Themes and click Customize to open Page Builder.
  2. In the left panel, find the Category Icons | PapaThemes widget.
  3. Drag it onto your page (the home page works well).
  4. Open the widget settings to add your categories and choose what each product card shows.

docs.text:

  • Reorder categories: drag an entry to change the order the tabs appear in.

docs.title: Tips
docs.text:
Tip

Use a consistent icon style across all tabs (same line weight and size) for a clean, professional row.

  • Double-check each category Path points to a real category on your store, or that tab will load empty.
  • Keep Product count under about 20 per category so the grid loads quickly.
  • Turn on Add to Cart or Quick View only if your product cards have room — they look best with "Always visible" left on.
  • Use the per-device Padding and Margin settings to keep tabs nicely spaced on phones as well as desktops.

docs.title: What's New
docs.text: Release notes for PapaThemes BigCommerce Widgets. Newest changes first.

docs.title: AI HTML Widget — Preview fix (v1.3.1)
docs.text: • Editing turns off in Preview. Switching Page Builder's Design / Preview toggle to Preview now fully disables the visual editor — clicking an element no longer makes its text editable or shows the floating toolbar. Links and buttons behave like the live storefront. Switch back to Design to edit again.

docs.title: AI HTML Widget — Visual Editor (v1.3.0)
docs.text:
• Click to edit, no mode switch needed. Clicking any element in your AI-generated section now opens the Design panel for it straight away — you no longer have to turn on Design Mode first. Links and buttons still work as normal when clicked.
  • A floating toolbar on the selected element. Right on the element you have chosen, a small toolbar lets you Duplicate it, move it Up or Down among its neighbours, or Delete it — all without asking the AI.
  • Automatic saving + Restore (replaces Save / Discard). Every change you make in the Design panel is now saved on its own — there are no more Save changes / Discard buttons. A Restore button appears once you have made an edit; click it to roll all of your design-panel changes back to the original look of the current AI version.
  • Element path breadcrumb. The panel header now shows the path of the element you are editing, listing its parent elements. Click any parent in the path to jump up and select it. Very long paths tuck themselves away behind a … that you can expand.
  • Layout controls for flexible and grid sections. When the element you select arranges its children in a row, column, or grid, a new Layout section appears with clear, icon-based controls for direction, wrapping, row and column gaps, and alignment — much like a professional design tool. Each can be set separately for Desktop, Tablet, and Mobile.
  • Cleaner editing across multiple widgets. If a page has more than one AI HTML widget, selecting an element in one now closes the other's panel, so the two no longer overlap.

docs.title: CKEditor Widget (v0.1.4)
docs.text:
• Fixed the stuck "Loading CKEditor library, please wait…" message. When a page had several CKEditor widgets, the loading notice could stay on screen forever. The editor now opens reliably even with many editors on the same page.
  • No more stray "Loading…" text saved into content. The loading overlay no longer gets captured into your saved content.
  • Lighter storefront pages. The editor only loads while you edit in Page Builder. Your live storefront and preview now render the content as plain HTML — no editor scripts, jQuery, or extra CSS — for faster page loads.

docs.title: AI HTML Widget — Visual Editor (v1.2.0)
docs.text:
• Edit your AI-generated sections visually, without chatting. Click any element inside Page Builder to open a docked Design panel on the right. Adjust text, padding and margin, colors, typography, borders, and alignment with familiar controls — the page updates as you type.
  • Responsive per breakpoint. Switch between Desktop, Tablet, and Mobile to fine-tune styles for each screen size. The active breakpoint follows your live preview width automatically.
  • Class-based editing. Edits update the element's shared style class. When a class is reused across repeated elements (e.g. cards in a grid), a small badge tells you how many elements your change will affect.
  • Save / Discard. Changes preview live; click Save changes to publish, or Discard to revert. A confirmation prompt protects you from leaving with unsaved edits.
  • Chat ⇄ Design toggle in the chatbot sidebar lets you flip between asking the AI and editing by hand.

docs.title: AI HTML Widget
docs.text: • Max tier upgraded to Claude Opus 4.7 — the highest-quality AI model is now powered by Anthropic's latest Opus release for sharper code generation, better long-context reasoning, and improved instruction-following.

docs.title: Infrastructure
docs.text: • Faster widget delivery via jsDelivr CDN — 19 widgets now load from jsDelivr with automatic S3 fallback. Expect lower latency for storefront visitors worldwide. No action required.

docs.title: Stability
docs.text:
• SMTP delivery hardening — welcome and renewal emails now retry transient failures automatically.
  • Credit promotion grant retry — improved resilience under DB contention.

docs.title: Dinosaur — Product Tags
docs.text: • Per-item style controls — set icon size, wave size, and wave color on individual product-tag items.

docs.title: AI HTML Widget
docs.text: • Compact conversation button — collapse long chat history into a summary to reduce API token costs while keeping context.

docs.title: Promotions
docs.text: • Free credit gift on first install — eligible new users automatically receive promotional credit. A one-time banner confirms the grant.

docs.title: AI HTML Widget
docs.text: • Image generation upgraded to Nano Banana 2 (Gemini Flash Image) — faster, higher-fidelity image generation for the chatbot's image-paste workflows.

docs.title: AI HTML Widget
docs.text: • Stop streaming + message queue — interrupt long AI responses mid-stream; queued messages send automatically when the current response finishes.

docs.title: AI HTML Widget
docs.text:
• Claude Sonnet upgraded from 4.5 to 4.6 — improvements to the Standard tier: more accurate code, better instruction-following.
  • Streaming dots animation keeps animating throughout the response.

docs.title: Eyeva — Banner Carousel
docs.text: • Version 1.0.1 — uses actual column values and floors slidesToScroll for correct layout on all breakpoints.

docs.title: AI HTML Widget
docs.text: • AI image generation in chatbot — generate images via prompt directly in the conversation.

docs.title: Section Templates
docs.text: • 22 new popup templates (263–284) — Page Builder compatible, with screenshots and API-compliant configuration.

docs.title: AI HTML Widget
docs.text:
• Welcome modal for first-time users.
  • Starter prompt buttons for quick actions.
  • First-time tooltip + module-based refactor.
  • Usage insights — understand how the widget is used.
  • Store data access — generated content can securely use your store's data.
  • Cleaner output — only the finished design is applied to your page.
  • More reliable long responses — handles lengthy answers and reviews them automatically.
  • Safer scripts — generated code runs in a protected sandbox, so a faulty snippet won't break your page.

docs.title: Documentation
docs.text:
• SEO — meta descriptions and FAQ added across all widget docs.
  • AI HTML Widget guide — screenshot illustrations added.

docs.title: Carousel Widget
docs.text:
Create a rotating carousel of images. It is ideal for brand logos, promotional banners, or any set of slides that link to pages on your store. Each slide is fully customizable and the carousel adapts to phones, tablets, and desktops.

Page Builder name: Carousel | PapaThemes Chiara — Version: 1.0.1

docs.title: Add it to your page
docs.text:
1. In your BigCommerce admin, open Storefront → My Themes and click Customize to open Page Builder.
  2. In the left panel, find the Carousel | PapaThemes Chiara widget.
  3. Drag it onto your page.
  4. The widget appears with several sample slides. Open the settings to swap in your own images and links.

docs.text:
This is the repeatable list of slides. Each entry is one image in the carousel.

  • Add a slide: click Add at the bottom of the Images list.
  • Remove a slide: open the slide entry and choose remove.
  • Reorder slides: drag an entry to change the order slides rotate in.

Each slide entry has these options in its Content tab, grouped into three sections.

docs.text:
Tip

Set a width and height on every slide so the carousel keeps a steady shape while images load.

  • Keep the carousel to around 10 slides or fewer for the smoothest experience.
  • Add Image alt text to each slide for better accessibility and SEO.
  • Leave Lazyload image on so the carousel does not slow down your page.
  • Use the Link field to send shoppers to a matching collection or landing page when they click a slide.

  4. The widget appears with three sample banners. Open the settings to add your own images, links, and text.

This is the repeatable list of banners. Each entry is one banner in the grid.

Tip

docs.title: Rich Text Editor (CKEditor) Widget
docs.text:
The Rich Text Editor widget lets you write and format content directly on your page using a familiar visual editor. Add headings, bold and italic text, lists, links, images, and tables without any code. Use it for product descriptions, blog-style content, policy pages, or any formatted text section.

Page Builder name: CKEditor | PapaThemes — Version: 0.1.3

docs.title: Add it to your page
docs.text:
1. In Page Builder, open the widget panel and find CKEditor | PapaThemes.
  2. Drag it onto the page where you want the text block to appear.
  3. The widget starts with sample content you can replace with your own.

docs.title: Edit your content
docs.text:
1. Turn on Edit Mode in Page Builder.
  2. Click inside the content area — the editing toolbar appears.
  3. Type your text and use the toolbar to format it.
  4. Save the page to keep your changes.

The visual toolbar gives you everything you need to format content:

  • Text styling: bold, italic, underline, strikethrough.
  • Paragraph styles: headings, paragraphs, and block quotes.
  • Lists: bulleted and numbered lists.
  • Links: add, edit, or remove links, with an option to open in a new tab.
  • Images: insert pictures, resize them, and add alt text or captions.
  • Tables: insert tables, add or remove rows and columns, and merge cells.
  • Alignment: left, center, right, or justify.

docs.title: General
docs.text: Option What it does Default Content The text shown in the block. Type and format it with the visual editor — you don't need to write any code. Sample content Show in container div Wraps the content in your theme's centered container so it lines up with the rest of the page width. Turn off to let it span the full available width. Off

docs.title: Tips
docs.text:
Format as you go

Use the toolbar's heading and list buttons instead of typing plain text. Proper headings and lists make your content easier to read and better for search engines.

Align with your layout

Turn on Show in container div when the text should match your theme's normal page margins. Leave it off for full-width sections like banners.

  • Add alt text to every image so your content stays accessible and SEO-friendly.
  • Use tables for comparisons or specs, but keep them simple so they read well on mobile.
  • Save the page after editing — your formatted content is stored with the page.

docs.title: Customer Reviews Widget
docs.text:
Showcase customer testimonials in an attractive, scrollable carousel. Each review can display an avatar, the reviewer's name, a title, the review text, a star rating, and a date — a great way to build trust with social proof.

Page Builder name: Customer Reviews | PapaThemes Beautify — Version: 1.0.1

docs.title: Add it to your page
docs.text:
1. In your BigCommerce admin, open Storefront → My Themes and click Customize to launch Page Builder.
  2. In the left Widgets panel, find Customer Reviews | PapaThemes Beautify.
  3. Drag it onto the spot on your page where you want the reviews to appear.
  4. The widget appears with four sample reviews. Click it to open the settings panel and edit them.

docs.title: Settings
docs.text: All settings live inside the Reviews list — there is one entry per review.

docs.title: Reviews
docs.text:
This is the repeatable list of reviews. Each entry is labeled Review.

  • Add a review with the Add button at the bottom of the list.
  • Remove a review using its delete icon.
  • Drag reviews by their handle to reorder them.

Each review has a Content tab with the options below.

Option What it does Default Avatar The picture beside each review. Choose a Type: Image (upload a photo), Color (a plain colored circle), or Hide. When Image or Color is used, you can also set the Width and Height in pixels; choosing Image lets you upload the photo, and Color lets you pick the circle color. Color, #e8e8e8, 92 × 92 px Title The short headline for the review (for example, "Only the best!"). Only the best! Content The full review text the customer left. You type this directly. Turn off for a wider, edge-to-edge layout. On

docs.title: Tips
docs.text:
Keep reviews consistent

Use a similar length for each review's text and the same avatar style across all entries so the carousel looks balanced.

  • The carousel shows three reviews at a time on desktop and one at a time on mobile, with swipe and dot navigation handled automatically.
  • Use square avatar images so they fit neatly inside the round avatar circle.
  • Put the customer's name and the date together in the Author field for a credible, dated testimonial.
  • Set Rating to Hide if you only want to show written testimonials without stars.

Display a row of eye-catching coupon tickets on any page. Each ticket can show a title and a short description, and you can give each one a coupon code or a link. When used with the Dinosaur theme, clicking a ticket applies its coupon code straight to the customer's cart.

  3. Drag it onto the spot where you want the coupons to appear.

  • Keep descriptions short so they fit neatly on one or two lines across all devices.
  • Use the Width Tablet and Mobile values to control how many tickets fit per row on smaller screens.
  • Turn on Custom title or description style only when you need fonts and colors that differ from your theme.

  • Keep all banner images the same width and height so the row lines up neatly.
  • Make sure you have more banners than the number of Columns, otherwise there is nothing to scroll to.
  • Add a short, action-focused Button label such as "Shop Now" or "View Deals" to each banner.
  • Turn on Show in .container element if you want the carousel aligned with the rest of your page content instead of full width.

docs.title: Tips

Always set the image first

docs.title: Getting Started: Install the App & Add Widgets
docs.text:
This guide walks you through everything you need to start using PapaThemes Widgets: install the app, turn on the widgets you want, then drag, drop, and configure them in Page Builder. No coding required.

Before you begin

You need a BigCommerce store and access to your store's admin dashboard (control panel). The PapaThemes Widgets app must be added to your store from the BigCommerce App Marketplace first.

docs.title: Step 1 — Open the app
docs.text: In your BigCommerce admin dashboard, go to Apps → My Apps, then click the PapaThemes Widgets app to open it.

docs.title: Step 2 — Install the widgets you want
docs.text:
Inside the app you'll see the Widget Manager — a list of every available widget with its installed version, the latest version, and an action button.

For each widget you can:

Button What it does Install Adds the widget to your store so it appears in Page Builder. Upgrade Updates an installed widget to the latest version (shown when a newer version is available). Uninstall Removes the widget from your store.

Click the button next to each widget you want. You only need to install the widgets you plan to use — you can always come back and add or remove more later.

Keep widgets up to date

When the Latest version is higher than your Installed version, click Upgrade to get the newest fixes and features.

docs.title: Step 3 — Add a widget to your page
docs.text:
Open Page Builder to preview your theme. In the left sidebar, on the Page Builder tab, the PapaThemes widgets now appear in their own group (look for the Custom widgets section).

Drag and drop any widget into an available region of your theme. The widget appears on the page right away with its default content, so you can see it immediately.

docs.title: Step 4 — Configure the widget
docs.text:
Click the widget you just added. Its settings panel opens on the left sidebar, where you can change colors, fonts, spacing, content, and every other option for that widget. Edits preview live on the page as you make them.

Find the settings for each widget

Every widget's options are explained in detail on its own documentation page — see Basic Widgets, AI Widgets, Beautify Widgets, and the theme-specific sections in the menu.

docs.title: Step 5 — Save and publish
docs.text:
When you're happy with the result:

  1. Click Save to keep your changes.
  2. Click Publish to make them live on your storefront.

docs.title: Tips
docs.text:
Try it on a draft first

Page Builder lets you edit safely before publishing. Make your changes, preview on desktop and mobile, then publish once everything looks right.

  • Install only the widgets you need — it keeps your widget list tidy.
  • Widgets added to a global region (such as the header or footer area) appear on every page; widgets added to a specific page appear only there.
  • Each widget is fully responsive, so preview both desktop and mobile views before publishing.

docs.title: Image Gallery Widget
docs.text:
The Image Gallery widget shows your images in a responsive grid. Shoppers can zoom an image in a full-screen lightbox, download it, and share it to Facebook, Twitter, or Pinterest. Use it for lookbooks, product showcases, portfolios, or any visual collection.

Option What it does Default Image The image shown in the gallery. Upload one or pick from your image library. Edit captions right on the page

You can click an image title directly on the page in Edit Mode and type your changes there.

docs.title: Tips
docs.text:
Plan your columns

The per-image Width settings control how many images sit side by side. For a tidy 4-column desktop gallery use 25% on desktop, 50% on tablet, and 100% on mobile. For 3 columns use about 33% on desktop.

Keep downloads fast

Optimize your images before uploading, and leave Enable Image Lazyload on so the page loads quickly even with many images.

  • Set Width and Height to match each image's real shape so cells don't look stretched.
  • Add a High-Res Image for Downloading when you want shoppers to grab a print-quality version while keeping the on-page image light.
  • Social sharing works best when your store and images are publicly accessible.

docs.title: Leaderboard Banner Widget

Display one wide, eye-catching banner with an image alongside a heading, description, and call-to-action button. It is ideal for sale announcements, seasonal campaigns, or highlighting a key collection across the top of a page or between sections.

Pick the right alignment

Tip

Enter only the path in the page URL field (for example /new-arrivals/), not the full address with your domain.

  • Make sure the URL points to a real page that actually lists products, otherwise the widget shows a short error message.
  • Keep the product limit reasonable (4–12 is a good range) so the section loads quickly.

docs.title: Responsive Widget
docs.text:
The Responsive widget controls whether the content that follows it is shown or hidden, based on the visitor's screen size and (optionally) whether they're logged in. Drop it on the page just before the layouts or widgets you want to control, then set rules for up to five screen-size ranges. In Edit Mode the widget shows a "Responsive Following" marker so you can always see where it sits.

Page Builder name: Responsive | PapaThemes — Version: 1.0.1

docs.title: Add it to your page
docs.text:
1. In Page Builder, open the widget panel and find Responsive | PapaThemes.
  2. Drag it onto the page directly above the content you want to show or hide.
  3. Open the settings panel and set your rules for each screen size.

docs.title: How it works
docs.text: The widget doesn't hold content itself — it acts as a control placed before other items. Using the Apply to setting you decide whether it affects only the next item or everything after it. For each of the five screen-size ranges you choose what should happen: show, hide, or leave the visibility unchanged.

docs.title: Screen Size 1 to Screen Size 5
docs.text:
There are five identical screen-size sections. Each one defines a range of screen widths and what to do for that range. Set Max to 0 to mean "no upper limit." Leave Display on Unset to make no change for that range.

Option What it does Default (Screen Size 1) Min The smallest screen width (in px) this range applies to. 0 Max The largest screen width (in px) this range applies to; use 0 for no upper limit. 550 Display What to do at this screen size: Show, Force Show, Hide, Force Hide, or Unset (no change). Unset

Default ranges for the five sections:

Section Min Max Screen Size 1 0 550 Screen Size 2 551 700 Screen Size 3 701 800 Screen Size 4 801 1260 Screen Size 5 1261 0 (no upper limit)

About the Display choices:

  • Show / Hide — shows or hides the content for that screen range.
  • Force Show / Force Hide — same as above but overrides other styles that might compete, useful when something stubbornly stays hidden or visible.
  • Unset — makes no change for that range, leaving the content's normal behavior in place.

docs.title: Apply
docs.text: Option What it does Default Apply to Which following content the rules affect: "All layouts & widgets following this widget" or "Only a layout or widget following this widget." All layouts & widgets following this widget Apply for login Limit the rules to certain visitors: None (everyone), Non logged in customers, Logged in customers, or Specific customer groups. None Customer group IDs (separated by comma) Appears when "Apply for login" is set to Specific customer groups. Enter the group IDs you want the rules to apply to, separated by commas. Empty

docs.title: Tips
docs.text:
Place it just before the content

The widget affects whatever comes after it, so position it immediately above the layout or widget you want to control.

Use Unset to leave a range alone

Only the ranges where you set Show or Hide will change. Leave the others on Unset so you don't accidentally affect screen sizes you didn't mean to.

  • Use "Only a layout or widget following this widget" when you want to control a single block, and "All layouts & widgets following this widget" to control everything below it.
  • Reach for Force Show or Force Hide when a normal Show/Hide doesn't take effect because of other theme styles.
  • Hidden content is still loaded by the browser, so use this for layout choices rather than to hide large videos or images you don't want downloaded.

docs.title: Section Templates Gallery
docs.text:
The AI HTML Widget comes with a growing library of ready-made section templates you can add to your BigCommerce store with a single click. Pick a template, drop it onto your page, then use the AI assistant to tweak the colors, text, images, and layout until it matches your brand.

This page showcases the Form Templates and Popup Templates collections. Many more templates — hero banners, product showcases, testimonials, and more — are available inside the Browse Blocks gallery in the AI assistant.

docs.title: How to Use a Template
docs.text:
1. Add the AI HTML Widget to your page in Page Builder.
  2. Open the AI assistant by clicking the purple AI button.
  3. Click Browse Blocks below the message box.
  4. Filter by category — for example Form Templates or Popup Templates — or search by name.
  5. Click a template to preview it with Desktop, Tablet, and Mobile views.
  6. Click Use This Block to add it to your page.
  7. Tell the assistant what to change, such as "Change the colors to match my brand."

For a full walkthrough, see the AI HTML Widget guide.

docs.title: Popup Templates
docs.text: A collection of conversion-focused popups for promotions, lead capture, compliance, and customer engagement. Each one is responsive and includes built-in timing — appearing after a delay, when a visitor is about to leave, or on a click — and remembers when a visitor has dismissed it so it does not keep reappearing.

docs.title: Marketing & Promotions
docs.text:
Drive sales with urgency-based promotional popups.

Welcome Discount Popup

First-time visitor welcome with a discount code and one-click copy. Appears after a short delay with a dimmed background.

Flash Sale Countdown Popup

Live countdown timer that creates urgency for limited-time offers, with a days, hours, minutes, and seconds display.

Free Shipping Bar

Floating top bar announcing your free shipping threshold. Stays visible and unobtrusive as customers browse.

Seasonal Promo Popup

Full-screen takeover for major seasonal campaigns — Black Friday, Christmas, summer sales — with high-impact visuals.

Exclusive Member Deal

VIP member-only offer with a premium gold-accent design. Creates exclusivity and rewards loyal customers.

Limited Stock Urgency

Slide-in notice showing a low stock count to encourage faster purchase decisions.

Purpose-built popups for common eCommerce scenarios.

Cart Reminder

Appears as a visitor is about to leave, reminding them of items still in their cart. Helps reduce abandoned checkouts.

Back in Stock Alert

docs.title: Customizing a Template
docs.text:
After you apply a template, use the AI assistant to make it your own. Just describe what you want in plain English. For example:

  • "Change the background color to #1a1a2e."
  • "Replace the placeholder text with our actual shipping policy."
  • "Update the discount code to SUMMER25."
  • "Show the popup after 10 seconds instead of 5."
  • "Add our logo to the popup header."

Tip

A template is just a starting point. The assistant can change anything — colors, fonts, layout, text, images, and timing. Describe what you want and it updates the template for you.

docs.title: Popup Timing
docs.text:
Popup templates appear in one of three ways, and you can ask the assistant to switch between them after applying a template.

Timing How it works Best for After a delay The popup appears a few seconds after the visitor arrives. Welcome offers, announcements On exit The popup appears when the visitor moves to leave the page. Last-chance offers, cart reminders On click The popup appears when a specific button or link is clicked. Size guides, store locators

Every popup also remembers when a visitor has closed it, so it won't keep popping up during their visit.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: One-time free credit gift for all users (old + new). Extensible for future promotions. Controlled via environment variables. Grant happens on /load (when user opens admin app).

docs.title: User model — new field
docs.text:
creditPromotions: [{
    code: String,            // unique promo identifier, e.g. 'welcome_5usd'
    amountCents: Number,     // amount granted, e.g. 500
    claimedAt: Date,         // when credit was granted
    noticeDismissedAt: Date, // null = chatbot notice not yet dismissed
}]

docs.title: ApiUsageLog — new enum value
docs.text: Add 'credit_promo' to subscriptionEvent enum. Log entry includes: - storeHash, subscriptionEvent: 'credit_promo' - amountCents, balanceBeforeCents, balanceAfterCents - Promo code stored via existing fields or log context

docs.title: Environment Variables
docs.text:
CREDIT_PROMO_CODE=welcome_5usd    # empty string or unset = feature disabled
CREDIT_PROMO_CENTS=500            # amount in cents ($5 = 500)

docs.title: /load endpoint (server/routers/load.js)
docs.text:
After verifyPayload + verifyUser, before building response:

  1. Read CREDIT_PROMO_CODE and CREDIT_PROMO_CENTS from env
  2. If code is empty or cents <= 0 → skip (feature disabled)
  3. Check if user.creditPromotions already contains entry with matching code
  4. If not claimed → atomic findOneAndUpdate: 
    User.findOneAndUpdate(
    {
        _id: user._id,
        'creditPromotions.code': { $ne: PROMO_CODE },
    },
    {
        $push: {
            creditPromotions: {
                code: PROMO_CODE,
                amountCents: PROMO_CENTS,
                claimedAt: new Date(),
                noticeDismissedAt: null,
            }
        },
        $inc: { creditBalance: PROMO_CENTS },
    },
    { new: true }
);
  5. If update succeeded → add creditPromoAwarded: { code, amountCents } to response
  6. Fire-and-forget log to ApiUsageLog with subscriptionEvent: 'credit_promo'

docs.title: Chatbot notice dismiss endpoint
docs.text:
New route or extend existing /ai routes:

POST /ai/dismiss-promo-notice
Body: { store_hash, code }

Sets noticeDismissedAt on the matching creditPromotions entry: 

User.findOneAndUpdate(
    { context: `stores/${storeHash}`, 'creditPromotions.code': code },
    { $set: { 'creditPromotions.$.noticeDismissedAt': new Date() } }
);

docs.title: Chatbot promo status
docs.text:
Extend existing chatbot data flow (e.g. subscription status check) to also return: 

{
    "activePromo": {
        "code": "welcome_5usd",
        "amountCents": 500,
        "noticeDismissed": false
    }
}

Only returned when a matching promo exists and noticeDismissedAt === null.

docs.title: Redux state
docs.text: fetchAuth response now may include creditPromoAwarded. Store in auth slice.

docs.title: Gift Banner
docs.text: When creditPromoAwarded is present in load response: - Show a top banner (styled differently from PromoBanner — gift/celebration theme) - Content: "You've received $X.XX free credits! Try our AI HTML Generator." - CTA "Use Now" button → setActiveTab('widgets') + setHighlightWidgetName('AI HTML Generator | PapaThemes') - Dismiss (X) button hides banner (session only, no persistence needed — it only shows on first load when credit is granted)

docs.title: Inline notice in chatbox
docs.text: When chatbot sidebar opens and state.messages.length === 0: 1. Fetch promo status from server (piggyback on existing subscription check or separate call) 2. If activePromo exists and !noticeDismissed: - Show inline notice via addInlineNotice(): "You have $X free credits! Start chatting to create HTML sections with AI." 3. When user sends first message: - Call POST /ai/dismiss-promo-notice (fire-and-forget) - Remove the inline notice element from DOM

docs.title: No localStorage
docs.text: All state is DB-backed. Once noticeDismissedAt is set, no chatbox instance will show the notice again.

docs.title: Flow Diagram
docs.text:
User opens admin app
  → POST /load
  → Server checks CREDIT_PROMO_CODE env
  → User.creditPromotions has code?
    ├─ No  → atomic grant + log → response includes creditPromoAwarded
    │        → Client shows gift banner with "Use Now" CTA
    └─ Yes → normal response

User opens chatbot sidebar
  → messages.length === 0?
    ├─ Yes → fetch promo status
    │        → activePromo && !noticeDismissed?
    │          ├─ Yes → show inline notice
    │          └─ No  → nothing
    └─ No  → nothing

User sends first message in chatbot
  → dismiss promo notice (fire-and-forget API call)
  → remove notice from DOM
  → all future chatbox opens: noticeDismissedAt is set → no notice

docs.title: Future Extensibility
docs.text: To add a new promotion: 1. Change env vars: CREDIT_PROMO_CODE=summer_2026, CREDIT_PROMO_CENTS=300 2. All users who haven't claimed summer_2026 will get it on next app load 3. Previous promotions remain in creditPromotions array (audit trail) 4. Each promo has independent notice dismiss state

docs.title: Credit Promotion System — Implementation Plan
docs.text:
For Claude: REQUIRED SUB-SKILL: Use superpowers:executing-plans to implement this plan task-by-task.

Goal: Grant one-time free credit to all users on app load, with admin banner and chatbot inline notice.

Architecture: Extensible creditPromotions[] array on User model, controlled by env vars. Server grants on /load, logs to ApiUsageLog. Chatbot reads promo status via subscription endpoint piggyback. Notice dismiss persisted in DB.

Tech Stack: Node.js/Express, MongoDB/Mongoose, React/Redux (admin app), vanilla JS Shadow DOM (chatbot widget)

docs.title: Task 4: Server — credit promo logic module
docs.text:
Files: - Create: server/lib/credit-promo.js

Step 1: Create the module

const mongoose = require('mongoose');
const User = mongoose.model('User');
const ApiUsageLog = require('../models/ApiUsageLog');
const logger = require('./logger');

const PROMO_CODE = (process.env.CREDIT_PROMO_CODE || '').trim();
const PROMO_CENTS = parseInt(process.env.CREDIT_PROMO_CENTS || '0', 10);

/**
 * Attempt to grant the active credit promo to a user (idempotent).
 * Returns { awarded: true, code, amountCents } if granted, or { awarded: false }.
 */
async function tryGrantPromo(user) {
    if (!PROMO_CODE || PROMO_CENTS <= 0) return { awarded: false };

    // Already claimed this promo?
    if (user.creditPromotions?.some(p => p.code === PROMO_CODE)) {
        return { awarded: false };
    }

    const balanceBefore = user.creditBalance || 0;

    const updated = await User.findOneAndUpdate(
        {
            _id: user._id,
            'creditPromotions.code': { $ne: PROMO_CODE },
        },
        {
            $push: {
                creditPromotions: {
                    code: PROMO_CODE,
                    amountCents: PROMO_CENTS,
                    claimedAt: new Date(),
                    noticeDismissedAt: null,
                },
            },
            $inc: { creditBalance: PROMO_CENTS },
        },
        { new: true }
    );

    if (!updated) return { awarded: false };

    // Fire-and-forget log
    const storeHash = user.context?.replace('stores/', '') || '';
    ApiUsageLog.create({
        storeHash,
        endpoint: 'admin-credit-adjustment',
        subscriptionEvent: 'credit_promo',
        amountCents: PROMO_CENTS,
        balanceBeforeCents: balanceBefore,
        balanceAfterCents: updated.creditBalance,
    }).catch(err => logger.error('CreditPromo.log', err));

    return { awarded: true, code: PROMO_CODE, amountCents: PROMO_CENTS };
}

/**
 * Get the active promo status for chatbot notice.
 * Returns { code, amountCents, noticeDismissed } or null.
 */
function getActivePromo(user) {
    if (!PROMO_CODE || PROMO_CENTS <= 0) return null;

    const entry = user.creditPromotions?.find(p => p.code === PROMO_CODE);
    if (!entry) return null;

    return {
        code: entry.code,
        amountCents: entry.amountCents,
        noticeDismissed: entry.noticeDismissedAt != null,
    };
}

/**
 * Dismiss the promo notice for a user (set noticeDismissedAt).
 */
async function dismissPromoNotice(storeHash, code) {
    await User.findOneAndUpdate(
        { context: `stores/${storeHash}`, 'creditPromotions.code': code },
        { $set: { 'creditPromotions.$.noticeDismissedAt': new Date() } }
    );
}

module.exports = { tryGrantPromo, getActivePromo, dismissPromoNotice };

docs.title: Compact Conversation Feature - Requirements & Acceptance Criteria
1. Problem Statement
docs.text:
As conversations grow longer, every subsequent message re-sends the entire conversation history to the Claude API. On a 20-message conversation, the input tokens can reach 100K+, costing the merchant significant credits per message. Today's auto-compaction only triggers when the context window is nearly full (~180K tokens), meaning merchants pay for inflated input tokens long before that threshold.

Merchant pain point: "I'm 15 messages in, my context stats show 80K tokens used, and each new message costs me ~$0.24 in input alone. I want to keep my conversation but stop paying for the full history on every call."

docs.title: 2. Target Persona
docs.text:
• Primary: Power users who build complex widgets over 10-30+ message sessions
  • Secondary: Cost-conscious merchants on small credit balances who want to extend their budget
  • Technical skill: Low to medium (the UI must make it obvious what compaction does)

docs.title: 3. Value Proposition
docs.text:
Aspect Current State After Compact Input tokens per message (20-msg session) ~80,000-120,000 ~5,000-15,000 Cost per follow-up message (Sonnet) ~\(0.24-\)0.36 ~\(0.015-\)0.045 Token savings None (until auto-compact) 70-90% reduction on input tokens Conversation context quality Full verbatim history Summarized key decisions + current code + recent messages

Core differentiator from existing auto-compact: User-initiated, happens much earlier in the conversation, and the user retains full visibility of their chat history in the UI.

docs.title: 4. How It Differs from Existing Auto-Compaction
docs.text:
The server already has compactMessages() in ai-token-manager.js which auto-triggers when context exceeds ~180K tokens. The new feature differs:

Dimension Existing Auto-Compact New User-Initiated Compact Trigger Automatic when near context limit User clicks a button Timing Very late (80%+ context used) Any time after first AI response Persistence Transient (only for that API call) Persisted -- subsequent messages use compacted version UI feedback None (invisible to user) Inline notice + context stats update Full history Lost (messages truncated/summarized in state) Preserved in UI and in DB

docs.title: US-1: Compact Button Appearance
docs.text:
As a merchant using the AI chatbot, I want to see a "Compact Conversation" tip after each AI response completes, so that I know I can reduce my token costs at any point.

Acceptance Criteria: 1. After each completed assistant message (not during streaming), a hint element appears below the message in the chat flow. 2. The hint displays: a compress/shrink icon + "Compact conversation to save tokens" (or similar copy). 3. The hint is styled as a subtle, non-intrusive tip (similar to the existing "Continue" button pattern -- light background, small text, icon + text + action button). 4. The hint does NOT appear after the very first assistant response (nothing to compact yet -- need at least 2 user+assistant exchanges). 5. The hint does NOT appear if the conversation is already compacted and no new messages have been added since. 6. The hint disappears when the user starts typing a new message or when they click compact. 7. If the conversation is already compacted and new messages are added, the hint reappears (re-compaction is possible).

docs.title: US-2: Compact Action Execution
docs.text:
As a merchant, I want clicking "Compact" to summarize my conversation history via the Claude API, so that future messages are cheaper without losing context quality.

Acceptance Criteria: 1. Clicking the compact button sends a request to a new server endpoint: POST /ai/compact-conversation. 2. The server uses claude-haiku-4-5 (cheapest model) to generate the summary, regardless of the user's selected model. 3. The summary prompt instructs Claude to preserve: - Key design decisions and requirements discussed - Current widget code (the latest <generated_code> block) - Any referenced image descriptions - User preferences expressed during the conversation - Error resolutions and fixes applied 4. The compacted result is a single [{ role: 'user', content: '<conversation_summary>...' }, { role: 'assistant', content: 'I understand...' }] message pair (matching existing hybridCompact format). 5. The compact API call costs credits (charged like any other AI call). The cost is shown to the user before confirming. 6. During compaction, a "Compacting conversation..." indicator appears (reuse thinking dots pattern). 7. If compaction fails (API error, insufficient credits), show an error notification and leave the conversation unchanged.

docs.title: US-3: Post-Compact Message Sending
docs.text:
As a merchant, I want messages sent after compacting to use ONLY the compacted summary (not full history) as context, so that I actually save tokens/credits.

Acceptance Criteria: 1. After compaction, state.compactedMessages holds the summary + any new messages added after compaction. 2. When sending to /ai/chat-pb, the request body's messages field contains ONLY state.compactedMessages (not state.messages). 3. The full state.messages array remains unchanged and is still used for UI display. 4. The /context stats endpoint reflects the reduced token count post-compaction. 5. Token usage reported after compaction should be significantly lower than before (testable via /context command).

docs.title: US-4: Full History Preservation in UI
docs.text:
As a merchant, I want to still see my complete conversation history in the chat UI after compacting, so that I don't lose any information visually.

Acceptance Criteria: 1. After compaction, all messages remain visible in the chat container -- no messages are removed or collapsed. 2. An inline notice appears in the chat flow at the compaction point: "Conversation compacted -- future messages will use a summarized context to save tokens." 3. Closing and reopening the chat widget shows the full message history (loaded from state.messages / DB). 4. The version history (code versions) is unaffected by compaction.

docs.title: US-5: Persistence of Compacted State
docs.text:
As a merchant, I want the compacted state to persist across chat sessions, so that reopening the chat doesn't lose the compaction benefit.

Acceptance Criteria: 1. The ChatHistory model stores a new field: compactedMessages (array, same structure as messages). 2. The ChatHistory model stores compactedAt (Date) indicating when last compaction occurred. 3. On saveChatHistory(), both messages (full) and compactedMessages (compacted) are saved. 4. On loadChatHistory(), if compactedMessages exists and is non-empty, it is loaded into state.compactedMessages. 5. For sessionStorage fallback (non-subscribers), compactedMessages is also saved/loaded.

docs.title: US-6: Re-Compaction and New Messages After Compact
docs.text:
As a merchant, I want to be able to compact again after adding more messages to a previously compacted conversation, so that I can keep costs low throughout a long session.

Acceptance Criteria: 1. After compaction, new user/assistant messages are appended to BOTH state.messages (full history) and state.compactedMessages. 2. If the user clicks compact again, a NEW compaction runs on state.compactedMessages (not full history -- this is cheaper because compactedMessages is already small + new messages). 3. The compaction hint reappears after 2+ new message exchanges post-compact. 4. There is no limit on how many times the user can compact. 5. Each re-compaction replaces the previous compactedMessages with a new summary.

docs.title: US-7: Context Stats Reflect Compaction
docs.text:
As a merchant, I want the /context command to show whether my conversation is compacted and the token savings, so that I can verify the cost reduction.

Acceptance Criteria: 1. The context stats panel shows a "Compaction" section with: - Status: "Active" (green) or "Not compacted" - Original message count vs. API message count - Estimated token savings (original tokens - compacted tokens) - Last compacted timestamp 2. The utilization bar reflects the compacted token count (not the full history count).

docs.title: POST /ai/compact-conversation
docs.text:
Request: 

{
    "store_hash": "abc123",
    "request_token": "...",
    "instanceId": "widget-xyz",

image descriptions referenced, errors encountered and resolved,

In the sendMessage function, at line ~291-302 where the fetch request is built:

// BEFORE (current):

docs.title: 9. Edge Cases
docs.text: Edge Case Handling User compacts with only 1 exchange Compact hint hidden; minimum 2 exchanges required Compaction API call fails Show error notification, state unchanged, hint remains Insufficient credits for compaction Show specific "insufficient credits" message with cost estimate User sends message during compaction Queue the message (existing queue system handles this) Conversation has images Summary preserves image descriptions but not base64 data; images remain in full history User does "New Chat" after compacting Both messages and compactedMessages are cleared Non-subscriber (sessionStorage) compactedMessages saved/loaded from sessionStorage alongside messages Very short conversation (< 5K tokens) Compact hint still appears but a warning: "Conversation is small -- compaction may not save significant tokens" Compacted state loaded but server changes compactedMessages treated as source of truth for API calls until next compact or new chat

docs.title: Scenario: 20-message widget-building session on Claude Sonnet
docs.text:
Without compaction: - Messages 1-10: Average ~50K input tokens per call = ~\(0.15/call - Messages 11-20: Average ~100K input tokens per call = ~\)0.30/call - Total input cost: ~$2.25

With compaction after message 10: - Messages 1-10: Same as above = ~\(0.15/call avg = ~\)1.50 - Compaction call (Haiku): ~5K input + 1.5K output = ~\(0.002 - Messages 11-20: Average ~15K input tokens per call = ~\)0.045/call - Total input cost: ~$1.50 + \(0.002 + ~\)0.45 = ~$1.95

Savings: ~$0.30 (13%) for one compaction, more with multiple compactions

For longer sessions (30+ messages) or conversations with images/code, savings compound to 30-50%.

docs.title: Phase 1 (MVP) -- Recommended Start
docs.text:
• Compact button as inline hint after assistant messages
  • Server endpoint using Haiku for summarization
  • Compacted state persisted in DB and sessionStorage
  • Basic inline notice at compaction point
  • Credit charge for compaction call

docs.title: Phase 2
docs.text:
• Context stats showing compaction savings
  • "Compact & Continue" option (compact + send queued message in one action)
  • Auto-suggest compaction when context reaches 50% utilization
  • Preview of summary before confirming compaction

docs.title: Phase 3
docs.text:
• Auto-compact preferences in admin settings (compact automatically at X% utilization)
  • Compaction quality feedback ("Was this summary accurate?")
  • Smart compaction that preserves more detail for code-heavy conversations

docs.title: 12. Success Metrics
docs.text: Metric Target Measurement Adoption rate 30% of 10+ message sessions use compact Track compact_conversation events in analytics Token savings 50%+ reduction in input tokens for subsequent messages Compare lastActualInputTokens before vs. after compaction Credit extension Merchants with compaction get 25%+ more messages per dollar Compare messages-per-credit for compacting vs. non-compacting sessions Conversation quality No increase in "the AI forgot context" complaints Monitor follow-up messages that reference earlier context Feature cost efficiency Compaction call costs < 5% of tokens saved Compare compaction API cost vs. cumulative input token savings

docs.title: 13. Technical Risks & Mitigations
docs.text: Risk Likelihood Impact Mitigation Summary loses critical context (e.g., specific CSS requirements) Medium High Include current code in summary prompt; keep last 4 messages verbatim alongside summary Compaction cost offsets savings for short conversations Low Low Only show hint after 2+ exchanges; show cost estimate before confirming Race condition: user sends message while compaction is in progress Medium Medium Set state.isCompacting flag; queue messages during compaction (reuse existing queue) Summary prompt itself is too large (very long conversations) Low Medium Truncate each message to 500 chars in summary prompt (matching existing summarizeMessages pattern) Compacted state diverges from full history after many messages Medium Low Re-compaction resolves this; show hint again after 5+ new exchanges

docs.title: 14. Open Questions for Product Decision
docs.text:
1. Should compaction be free for the first use per session? This could encourage adoption without merchants worrying about paying to save money.
  2. Should we show a preview of the summary before applying? Adds UX complexity but gives merchants confidence. Recommendation: Phase 2.
  3. Should the compact hint auto-dismiss after a timeout? Risk of being annoying vs. being discoverable. Recommendation: Dismiss on next user input, no auto-timeout.
  4. Should compaction preserve the exact latest code block verbatim (not summarized)? Recommendation: Yes -- always include the full current code in the compacted context.

docs.title: AI HTML Widget — Visual Editor Implementation Plan
docs.text:
For agentic workers: REQUIRED SUB-SKILL: Use superpowers:subagent-driven-development (recommended) or superpowers:executing-plans to implement this plan task-by-task. Steps use checkbox (- [ ]) syntax for tracking.

Goal: Add a WYSIWYG, class-based visual style editor to the AI HTML Generator | PapaThemes widget so merchants can edit text/spacing/color/typography/border/alignment per breakpoint inside Page Builder, with live edits committed only on "Save changes".

Architecture: A "Design mode" added to the existing chatbot Shadow-DOM sidebar. Selecting an element resolves its UUID-prefixed class; edits mutate the matching CSSOM rule inside the live <style> (base for Desktop, @media for Tablet/Mobile) — no sendWidgetUpdate per edit. On Save, the content markup is serialized once and persisted through the existing widget pipeline. Active breakpoint auto-follows the live viewport width.

Tech Stack: Vanilla ES6 modules (client widgets), Vite build, Vitest + jsdom for unit tests, Playwright for E2E. No framework in the storefront widget.

Spec: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-25-ai-html-visual-editor-design.md Mockup (reference for panel markup/styles): docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-25-ai-html-visual-editor-mockup.html

docs.title: Repo constraints (read before coding)
docs.text:
• Write-hook blocks the literal token inner+HTML. When Claude authors files, that exact token in source is rejected. Use the helpers readInner(el) / writeInner(el, html) defined in Task 6 (dom-html.js) which access the property via bracket notation. Never type the literal token in any new file.
  • Coding style: 4-space indent, single quotes, import/require at top of file, brace on same line.
  • Editor runs only in Page Builder editor mode (isEditorMode), same gating as the chatbot.
  • AI output contract (server/lib/ai-system-prompt.js): every element has a UUID-prefixed class, styles live in a <style> block, no inline styles.
