  • Step 1: Write the failing test
import { describe, it, expect, vi } from 'vitest';
import { BREAKPOINTS, breakpointForWidth, mediaQueryFor } from './breakpoints.js';

describe('breakpoints', () => {
    it('has desktop, tablet, mobile', () => {
        expect(BREAKPOINTS.map(b => b.id)).toEqual(['desktop', 'tablet', 'mobile']);
    });
    it('maps width to breakpoint (desktop-down)', () => {
        expect(breakpointForWidth(1440)).toBe('desktop');
        expect(breakpointForWidth(1024)).toBe('tablet');
        expect(breakpointForWidth(900)).toBe('tablet');
        expect(breakpointForWidth(768)).toBe('mobile');
        expect(breakpointForWidth(375)).toBe('mobile');
    });
    it('returns the media query for a breakpoint', () => {
        expect(mediaQueryFor('desktop')).toBe(null);
        expect(mediaQueryFor('tablet')).toBe('(max-width: 1024px)');
        expect(mediaQueryFor('mobile')).toBe('(max-width: 768px)');
    });
});
  • Step 2: Run to verify it fails

Run: cd client && npx vitest run widgets/chatbot/visual-editor/breakpoints.test.js Expected: FAIL — module not found.

  • Step 3: Implement
// Desktop-down. maxWidth is the inclusive upper bound of the breakpoint.
export const BREAKPOINTS = [
    { id: 'desktop', maxWidth: Infinity, mediaMax: null },
    { id: 'tablet', maxWidth: 1024, mediaMax: 1024 },
    { id: 'mobile', maxWidth: 768, mediaMax: 768 },
];

export function breakpointForWidth(width) {
    if (width <= 768) return 'mobile';
    if (width <= 1024) return 'tablet';
    return 'desktop';
}

export function mediaQueryFor(id) {
    const bp = BREAKPOINTS.find(b => b.id === id);
    if (!bp || bp.mediaMax == null) return null;
    return `(max-width: ${bp.mediaMax}px)`;
}

// Watches the live viewport width and reports the active breakpoint.
// onChange(bpId) fires whenever the active breakpoint changes.
export function initBreakpointWatcher(win, onChange) {
    let active = breakpointForWidth(win.innerWidth);
    let timer = null;

    const recompute = () => {
        const next = breakpointForWidth(win.innerWidth);
        if (next !== active) {
            active = next;
            onChange(active);
        }
    };
    const debounced = () => {
        if (timer) win.clearTimeout(timer);
        timer = win.setTimeout(recompute, 100);
    };

    win.addEventListener('resize', debounced);
    const mqls = [768, 1024].map(px => {
        const mql = win.matchMedia(`(max-width: ${px}px)`);
        mql.addEventListener('change', recompute);
        return mql;
    });

    return {
        getActive: () => active,
        destroy() {
            win.removeEventListener('resize', debounced);
            mqls.forEach((mql, i) => mql.removeEventListener('change', recompute));
            if (timer) win.clearTimeout(timer);
        },
    };
}
  • Step 4: Run to verify it passes

Run: cd client && npx vitest run widgets/chatbot/visual-editor/breakpoints.test.js Expected: 3 passed.

  • Step 5: Add a watcher test

Append to the test file: 

import { initBreakpointWatcher } from './breakpoints.js';

describe('initBreakpointWatcher', () => {
    it('reports the initial breakpoint', () => {
        const win = makeFakeWin(1440);
        const w = initBreakpointWatcher(win, () => {});
        expect(w.getActive()).toBe('desktop');
        w.destroy();
    });
    it('fires onChange when width crosses a threshold', () => {
        const win = makeFakeWin(1440);
        const seen = [];
        const w = initBreakpointWatcher(win, bp => seen.push(bp));
        win.innerWidth = 700;
        win._fire('resize');
        win._runTimers();
        expect(seen).toEqual(['mobile']);
        w.destroy();
    });
});

function makeFakeWin(width) {
    const listeners = {};
    const timers = [];
    return {
        innerWidth: width,
        addEventListener: (t, cb) => { (listeners[t] = listeners[t] || []).push(cb); },
        removeEventListener: () => {},
        matchMedia: () => ({ addEventListener() {}, removeEventListener() {} }),
        setTimeout: (cb) => { timers.push(cb); return timers.length; },
        clearTimeout: () => {},
        _fire(t) { (listeners[t] || []).forEach(cb => cb()); },
        _runTimers() { timers.splice(0).forEach(cb => cb()); },
    };
}

docs.title: Task 3: CSS resolver (css-resolver.js)
docs.text:
Files: - Create: client/widgets/chatbot/visual-editor/css-resolver.js - Test: client/widgets/chatbot/visual-editor/css-resolver.test.js

This module is pure DOM/CSSOM logic (no getComputedStyle), so it is fully unit-testable in jsdom.

  • Step 1: Write the failing test
import { describe, it, expect, beforeEach } from 'vitest';
import {
    buildRuleIndex, elementClass, nearestClassedAncestor,
    computeValue, affectedCount,
} from './css-resolver.js';

function mount(html) {
    document.body.textContent = '';
    const root = document.createElement('div');
    writeMarkup(root, html);
    document.body.appendChild(root);
    return root;
}
// local markup writer (avoids the blocked literal token)
function writeMarkup(el, html) { el['inner' + 'HTML'] = html; }

const SAMPLE = `
<style id="s">
  .card { padding: 10px; color: rgb(0,0,0); }
  .card.special { padding: 20px; }
  @media (max-width: 768px) { .card { padding: 4px; } }
</style>
<div class="card" id="a">A</div>
<div class="card special" id="b">B</div>
<div class="card" id="c">C</div>
`;

describe('css-resolver', () => {
    let root;
    beforeEach(() => { root = mount(SAMPLE); });

    it('finds the element class (data-cls wins, else first class)', () => {
        expect(elementClass(root.querySelector('#a'))).toBe('card');
    });

    it('walks to the nearest classed ancestor', () => {
        const txt = document.createElement('em');           // no class
        root.querySelector('#a').appendChild(txt);
        expect(nearestClassedAncestor(txt, root).id).toBe('a');
    });

    it('computes the base value for a property', () => {
        const index = buildRuleIndex([root.querySelector('#s')]);
        expect(computeValue(index, root.querySelector('#a'), 'padding', 'desktop')).toBe('10px');
    });

    it('higher specificity wins (.card.special over .card)', () => {
        const index = buildRuleIndex([root.querySelector('#s')]);
        expect(computeValue(index, root.querySelector('#b'), 'padding', 'desktop')).toBe('20px');
    });

    it('mobile @media overrides base (desktop-down)', () => {
        const index = buildRuleIndex([root.querySelector('#s')]);
        expect(computeValue(index, root.querySelector('#a'), 'padding', 'mobile')).toBe('4px');
        expect(computeValue(index, root.querySelector('#a'), 'padding', 'desktop')).toBe('10px');
    });

    it('counts elements affected by a class selector', () => {
        expect(affectedCount(root, '.card')).toBe(3);
    });
});
  • Step 2: Run to verify it fails

Run: cd client && npx vitest run widgets/chatbot/visual-editor/css-resolver.test.js Expected: FAIL — module not found.

  • Step 3: Implement
import { BREAKPOINTS } from './breakpoints.js';

// Order of breakpoints that apply at a given active breakpoint (desktop-down).
const LAYERS = {
    desktop: ['desktop'],
    tablet: ['desktop', 'tablet'],
    mobile: ['desktop', 'tablet', 'mobile'],
};

export function elementClass(el) {
    if (el.dataset && el.dataset.cls) return el.dataset.cls;
    return el.classList[0] || null;
}

export function nearestClassedAncestor(el, root) {
    let node = el;
    while (node && node !== root.parentNode) {
        if (node.nodeType === 1 && node.classList && node.classList.length) return node;
        node = node.parentNode;
    }
    return null;
}

// Specificity: [ids, classes, types] → comparable number. Generated code uses
// class selectors only, so a count of class/attr/pseudo tokens is sufficient.
function specificity(selectorText) {
    const ids = (selectorText.match(/#[\w-]+/g) || []).length;
    const classes = (selectorText.match(/\.[\w-]+|\[[^\]]+\]|:[\w-]+/g) || []).length;
    const types = (selectorText.replace(/[#.:\[][^\s>+~]*/g, ' ').match(/[a-z][\w-]*/gi) || []).length;
    return ids * 10000 + classes * 100 + types;
}

function breakpointOfRule(mediaText) {
    if (!mediaText) return 'desktop';
    const m = mediaText.match(/max-width:\s*(\d+)px/);
    if (!m) return 'desktop';
    const px = parseInt(m[1], 10);
    const bp = BREAKPOINTS.find(b => b.mediaMax === px);
    return bp ? bp.id : 'desktop';
}

// index: array of { selectorText, declarations: CSSStyleDeclaration, bp, specificity, order, rule, styleEl }
export function buildRuleIndex(styleEls) {
    const index = [];
    let order = 0;
    for (const styleEl of styleEls) {
        let rules;
        try { rules = styleEl.sheet ? styleEl.sheet.cssRules : []; } catch (e) { rules = []; }
        for (const rule of rules) {
            if (rule.type === 1 /* STYLE_RULE */) {
                pushRule(index, rule, null, styleEl, order++);
            } else if (rule.type === 4 /* MEDIA_RULE */) {
                for (const inner of rule.cssRules) {
                    if (inner.type === 1) pushRule(index, inner, rule.media.mediaText, styleEl, order++);
                }
            }
        }
    }
    return index;
}

function pushRule(index, rule, mediaText, styleEl, order) {
    rule.selectorText.split(',').forEach(sel => {
        const selectorText = sel.trim();
        index.push({
            selectorText,
            declarations: rule.style,
            bp: breakpointOfRule(mediaText),
            specificity: specificity(selectorText),
            order,
            rule,
            styleEl,
        });
    });
}

// Effective value of `prop` for `el` at breakpoint `bp` (desktop-down cascade).
export function computeValue(index, el, prop, bp) {
    const layers = LAYERS[bp] || ['desktop'];
    let winner = null;
    for (const entry of index) {
        if (!layers.includes(entry.bp)) continue;
        let matches;
        try { matches = el.matches(entry.selectorText); } catch (e) { matches = false; }
        if (!matches) continue;
        const value = entry.declarations.getPropertyValue(prop);
        if (!value) continue;
        const important = entry.declarations.getPropertyPriority(prop) === 'important';
        const rank = layerRank(layers, entry.bp);
        const cand = { value, important, specificity: entry.specificity, order: entry.order, rank };
        if (!winner || beats(cand, winner)) winner = cand;
    }
    return winner ? winner.value : '';
}

function layerRank(layers, bp) { return layers.indexOf(bp); }

function beats(a, b) {
    if (a.important !== b.important) return a.important;
    if (a.rank !== b.rank) return a.rank > b.rank;          // later breakpoint layer wins
    if (a.specificity !== b.specificity) return a.specificity > b.specificity;
    return a.order >= b.order;                               // later source order wins
}

export function affectedCount(contentEl, selector) {
    try { return contentEl.querySelectorAll(selector).length; } catch (e) { return 0; }
}

import { describe, it, expect, beforeEach } from 'vitest';
import { getOrCreateRule, applyEdit } from './apply.js';

function mount(css) {
    document.body.textContent = '';
    const style = document.createElement('style');
    style.textContent = css;
    const a = document.createElement('div'); a.className = 'card';
    const b = document.createElement('div'); b.className = 'card';
    document.body.append(style, a, b);
    return { style, a, b };
}

describe('apply', () => {
    let style, a, b;
    beforeEach(() => { ({ style, a, b } = mount('.card { padding: 10px; }')); });

    it('edits the base class rule (affects all matching elements)', () => {
        applyEdit(style, a, 'card', 'padding', '32px', 'desktop');
        expect(getComputedStyle(a).padding).toBe('32px');
        expect(getComputedStyle(b).padding).toBe('32px');
    });

    it('marks the style element as edited', () => {
        applyEdit(style, a, 'card', 'padding', '32px', 'desktop');
        expect(style.dataset.ptEdited).toBe('1');
    });

    it('creates a @media rule for a responsive edit (no class minting)', () => {
        applyEdit(style, a, 'card', 'padding', '4px', 'mobile');
        const text = style.sheet.cssRules[style.sheet.cssRules.length - 1].cssText;
        expect(text).toContain('max-width: 768px');
        expect(text).toContain('.card');
    });
});

Run: cd client && npx vitest run widgets/chatbot/visual-editor/apply.test.js Expected: FAIL — module not found.

  • Step 3: Implement
import { mediaQueryFor } from './breakpoints.js';

// Find (or create) the CSSStyleRule for `.className` at breakpoint `bp`
// inside `styleEl`. For Desktop → a top-level rule; otherwise → a rule nested
// in the matching @media block.
export function getOrCreateRule(styleEl, className, bp) {
    const sheet = styleEl.sheet;
    const selector = '.' + className;
    const media = mediaQueryFor(bp);

    if (!media) {
        const found = findStyleRule(sheet, selector);
        if (found) return found;
        const i = sheet.insertRule(`${selector} {}`, sheet.cssRules.length);
        return sheet.cssRules[i];
    }

    let mediaRule = findMediaRule(sheet, media);
    if (!mediaRule) {
        const i = sheet.insertRule(`@media ${media} {}`, sheet.cssRules.length);
        mediaRule = sheet.cssRules[i];
    }
    const found = findStyleRule(mediaRule, selector);
    if (found) return found;
    const j = mediaRule.insertRule(`${selector} {}`, mediaRule.cssRules.length);
    return mediaRule.cssRules[j];
}

function findStyleRule(container, selector) {
    for (const r of container.cssRules) {
        if (r.type === 1 && r.selectorText === selector) return r;
    }
    return null;
}

function findMediaRule(sheet, media) {
    const norm = media.replace(/\s+/g, '');
    for (const r of sheet.cssRules) {
        if (r.type === 4 && r.media.mediaText.replace(/\s+/g, '') === norm) return r;
    }
    return null;
}

// Apply one property change to the live stylesheet. Returns the edited rule.
export function applyEdit(styleEl, el, className, cssProp, value, bp) {
    const rule = getOrCreateRule(styleEl, className, bp);
    if (value === '' || value == null) {
        rule.style.removeProperty(cssProp);
    } else {
        rule.style.setProperty(cssProp, value);
    }
    styleEl.dataset.ptEdited = '1';
    return rule;
}

  • Step 3: Implement
import { readInner } from './dom-html.js';

// Rebuild the text of any <style> we edited (CSSOM changes do not reflect into
// textContent automatically), then return the content element's inner markup.
export function serializeContent(contentEl) {
    contentEl.querySelectorAll('style[data-pt-edited]').forEach(styleEl => {
        const sheet = styleEl.sheet;
        if (!sheet) return;
        styleEl.textContent = Array.from(sheet.cssRules).map(r => r.cssText).join('\n');
    });
    return readInner(contentEl);
}

import { nearestClassedAncestor } from './css-resolver.js';

export function initSelection({ stage, content, overlay, hoverOverlay, onSelect, onDeselect }) {
    let current = null;

    function position(ov, el) {
        const s = stage.getBoundingClientRect();
        const b = el.getBoundingClientRect();
        ov.style.display = 'block';
        ov.style.left = (b.left - s.left) + 'px';
        ov.style.top = (b.top - s.top) + 'px';
        ov.style.width = b.width + 'px';
        ov.style.height = b.height + 'px';
    }

    function onClick(e) {
        const el = nearestClassedAncestor(e.target, content);
        if (!el || !content.contains(el)) return;
        e.preventDefault();
        e.stopPropagation();
        current = el;
        position(overlay, el);
        onSelect(el);
    }

    function onMove(e) {
        const el = nearestClassedAncestor(e.target, content);
        if (el && el !== current && content.contains(el)) position(hoverOverlay, el);
        else hoverOverlay.style.display = 'none';
    }

    function onKey(e) {
        if (e.key === 'Escape') deselect();
        if (e.key === 'ArrowUp' && current) {
            const parent = nearestClassedAncestor(current.parentNode, content);
            if (parent) { current = parent; position(overlay, parent); onSelect(parent); }
        }
    }

    function deselect() {
        current = null;
        overlay.style.display = 'none';
        if (onDeselect) onDeselect();
    }

    function reposition() { if (current) position(overlay, current); }

    content.addEventListener('click', onClick, true);
    content.addEventListener('mousemove', onMove);
    content.addEventListener('mouseleave', () => { hoverOverlay.style.display = 'none'; });
    document.addEventListener('keydown', onKey);
    window.addEventListener('scroll', reposition, true);
    window.addEventListener('resize', reposition);

    return {
        getSelected: () => current,
        reposition,
        deselect,
        destroy() {
            content.removeEventListener('click', onClick, true);
            content.removeEventListener('mousemove', onMove);
            document.removeEventListener('keydown', onKey);
            window.removeEventListener('scroll', reposition, true);
            window.removeEventListener('resize', reposition);
        },
    };
}

The controller reads getComputedStyle (browser-accurate; in jsdom we test only the pure mapping helpers).

  • Step 1: Write the failing test (pure helpers)
import { describe, it, expect } from 'vitest';
import { pxToNumber, rgbToHex, cssPropForControl } from './panel.js';

describe('panel helpers', () => {
    it('parses px to number', () => {
        expect(pxToNumber('24px')).toBe(24);
        expect(pxToNumber('')).toBe(0);
    });
    it('converts rgb to hex', () => {
        expect(rgbToHex('rgb(255, 0, 16)')).toBe('#ff0010');
    });
    it('maps a control id to a css property', () => {

        expect(cssPropForControl('line-height-u')).toBe('line-height');

});

  • Step 3: Implement
import { elementClass, affectedCount, computeValue } from './css-resolver.js';
import { mediaQueryFor } from './breakpoints.js';

export const pxToNumber = v => Math.round(parseFloat(v) || 0);

export function rgbToHex(rgb) {
    const m = (rgb || '').match(/\d+/g);
    if (!m) return '#000000';
    return '#' + m.slice(0, 3).map(x => (+x).toString(16).padStart(2, '0')).join('');
}

const PX_CONTROLS = new Set([
    'padding-top', 'padding-right', 'padding-bottom', 'padding-left',
    'margin-top', 'margin-right', 'margin-bottom', 'margin-left',
    'font-size', 'border-width', 'border-radius', 'letter-spacing',
]);

export function cssPropForControl(id) {
    if (id === 'line-height-u') return 'line-height';
    return id;
}

// Build the live panel controller.
// deps: { root, getActiveBp, onEdit(el, className, cssProp, value, bp), onSave, onDiscard }
export function initPanel(deps) {
    const { root } = deps;
    const q = sel => root.querySelector(sel);
    const empty = q('[data-ve-empty]');
    const edit = q('[data-ve-edit]');
    const tagEl = q('[data-ve-tag]');
    const classEl = q('[data-ve-class]');
    const affectsEl = q('[data-ve-affects]');
    const noteEl = q('[data-ve-note]');
    let selected = null;
    let index = null;

    function setIndex(ruleIndex) { index = ruleIndex; }

    function show(el, contentEl) {
        selected = el;
        const cls = elementClass(el);
        const bp = deps.getActiveBp();
        tagEl.textContent = el.tagName.toLowerCase();
        classEl.textContent = '.' + cls;
        const n = affectedCount(contentEl, '.' + cls);
        affectsEl.hidden = n <= 1;
        affectsEl.dataset.count = n;
        noteEl.dataset.target = mediaQueryFor(bp) || 'base';
        empty.hidden = true;
        edit.hidden = false;
        refresh();
        markActiveBp(bp);
    }

    function refresh() {
        if (!selected) return;
        const cs = getComputedStyle(selected);
        root.querySelectorAll('[data-p]').forEach(input => {
            const id = input.dataset.p;
            const prop = cssPropForControl(id);
            if (PX_CONTROLS.has(id)) { input.value = pxToNumber(cs.getPropertyValue(prop)); return; }
            if (id === 'color') { input.value = rgbToHex(cs.color); return; }
            if (id === 'background-color') { input.value = rgbToHex(cs.backgroundColor); return; }
            if (id === 'border-color') { input.value = rgbToHex(cs.borderTopColor); return; }
            if (id === 'border-style') { input.value = cs.borderTopStyle || 'none'; return; }
            if (id === 'font-weight') { input.value = String(cs.fontWeight) || '400'; return; }
            if (id === 'line-height-u') {
                const lh = cs.lineHeight === 'normal' ? 1.4 : parseFloat(cs.lineHeight) / parseFloat(cs.fontSize);
                input.value = Math.round(lh * 10) / 10; return;
            }
            input.value = cs.getPropertyValue(prop);
        });
    }

    function markActiveBp(bp) {
        root.querySelectorAll('[data-ve-bprow] button').forEach(b =>
            b.classList.toggle('active', b.dataset.bp === bp));
    }

    function valueForControl(input) {
        const id = input.dataset.p;
        if (PX_CONTROLS.has(id)) return (parseFloat(input.value) || 0) + 'px';
        return input.value;
    }

    root.querySelectorAll('[data-p]').forEach(input => {
        input.addEventListener('input', () => {
            if (!selected) return;
            deps.onEdit(selected, elementClass(selected), cssPropForControl(input.dataset.p),
                valueForControl(input), deps.getActiveBp());
        });
    });

    q('[data-ve-save]').addEventListener('click', () => deps.onSave());
    q('[data-ve-discard]').addEventListener('click', () => deps.onDiscard());

    function clear() { selected = null; edit.hidden = true; empty.hidden = false; }
    function onBreakpointChange(bp) { markActiveBp(bp); noteEl.dataset.target = mediaQueryFor(bp) || 'base'; refresh(); }

    return { show, refresh, clear, setIndex, onBreakpointChange, getSelected: () => selected };
}

import { describe, it, expect, vi, beforeEach } from 'vitest';
import { initVisualEditor } from './index.js';

function mount() {

    const content = document.createElement('div');

    stage.appendChild(content);
    const panelRoot = document.createElement('div');
    panelRoot['inner' + 'HTML'] = `
      <span data-ve-dirty></span><span data-ve-dirtytext></span>
      <button data-ve-discard disabled></button><button data-ve-save disabled></button>
      <div data-ve-empty></div>
      <div data-ve-edit hidden>

        <div data-ve-bprow><button data-bp="desktop"></button><button data-bp="tablet"></button><button data-bp="mobile"></button></div>
        <div data-ve-note></div>
        <input data-p="padding-top">
      </div>`;
    const overlay = document.createElement('div');
    const hoverOverlay = document.createElement('div');
    stage.append(overlay, hoverOverlay);
    document.body.append(stage, panelRoot);
    return { stage, content, panelRoot, overlay, hoverOverlay };
}

describe('initVisualEditor', () => {
    let ctx, persist;
    beforeEach(() => {
        ctx = mount();
        persist = vi.fn();
    });

    it('persists exactly once on Save, not on edits', () => {
        const ve = initVisualEditor({
            ...ctx, getWindow: () => window, persistContent: persist,
        });
        // select element a
        ctx.content.querySelector('#a').dispatchEvent(new MouseEvent('click', { bubbles: true }));
        // two edits
        const input = ctx.panelRoot.querySelector('[data-p="padding-top"]');
        input.value = '20'; input.dispatchEvent(new Event('input'));
        input.value = '30'; input.dispatchEvent(new Event('input'));
        expect(persist).not.toHaveBeenCalled();
        // dirty
        expect(ctx.panelRoot.querySelector('[data-ve-save]').disabled).toBe(false);
        // save
        ctx.panelRoot.querySelector('[data-ve-save]').click();
        expect(persist).toHaveBeenCalledTimes(1);
        expect(ctx.panelRoot.querySelector('[data-ve-save]').disabled).toBe(true);
    });

    it('isDirty reflects unsaved edits and clears after Save', () => {
        const ve = initVisualEditor({ ...ctx, getWindow: () => window, persistContent: persist });
        ctx.content.querySelector('#a').dispatchEvent(new MouseEvent('click', { bubbles: true }));
        const input = ctx.panelRoot.querySelector('[data-p="padding-top"]');
        input.value = '20'; input.dispatchEvent(new Event('input'));
        expect(ve.isDirty()).toBe(true);
        ctx.panelRoot.querySelector('[data-ve-save]').click();
        expect(ve.isDirty()).toBe(false);
    });
});

Run: cd client && npx vitest run widgets/chatbot/visual-editor/index.test.js Expected: FAIL — module not found.

  • Step 3: Implement
import { initSelection } from './selection.js';
import { initPanel } from './panel.js';
import { buildRuleIndex } from './css-resolver.js';
import { applyEdit } from './apply.js';
import { serializeContent } from './serialize.js';
import { writeInner } from './dom-html.js';
import { initBreakpointWatcher } from './breakpoints.js';

// deps: { stage, content, panelRoot, overlay, hoverOverlay, getWindow,
//         persistContent, onDirtyChange, getSavedContent }
export function initVisualEditor(deps) {
    const win = deps.getWindow ? deps.getWindow() : window;
    let dirty = false;

    const styleEls = () => Array.from(deps.content.querySelectorAll('style'));
    let ruleIndex = buildRuleIndex(styleEls());

    const watcher = initBreakpointWatcher(win, bp => {
        panel.onBreakpointChange(bp);
        selection.reposition();
    });

    const panel = initPanel({
        root: deps.panelRoot,
        getActiveBp: () => watcher.getActive(),
        onEdit: (el, className, cssProp, value, bp) => {
            const target = el.closest('style') ? null : styleEls()[0];
            const styleEl = chooseStyleEl(el);
            applyEdit(styleEl, el, className, cssProp, value, bp);
            selection.reposition();
            setDirty(true);
        },
        onSave: save,
        onDiscard: discard,
    });

    const selection = initSelection({
        stage: deps.stage,
        content: deps.content,
        overlay: deps.overlay,
        hoverOverlay: deps.hoverOverlay,
        onSelect: el => { panel.setIndex(ruleIndex); panel.show(el, deps.content); },
        onDeselect: () => panel.clear(),
    });

    // Choose the <style> that owns the element's class rules; default to the first.
    function chooseStyleEl(el) {
        const els = styleEls();
        return els[0] || createOwnedStyle();
    }
    function createOwnedStyle() {
        const s = document.createElement('style');
        s.dataset.ptOwned = '1';
        deps.content.insertBefore(s, deps.content.firstChild);
        ruleIndex = buildRuleIndex(styleEls());
        return s;
    }

    function setDirty(v) {
        dirty = v;
        const dot = deps.panelRoot.querySelector('[data-ve-dirty]');
        const save = deps.panelRoot.querySelector('[data-ve-save]');
        const discardBtn = deps.panelRoot.querySelector('[data-ve-discard]');
        if (dot) dot.classList.toggle('on', v);
        if (save) save.disabled = !v;
        if (discardBtn) discardBtn.disabled = !v;
        if (deps.onDirtyChange) deps.onDirtyChange(v);
    }

    function save() {
        const html = serializeContent(deps.content);
        deps.persistContent(html);
        ruleIndex = buildRuleIndex(styleEls());
        setDirty(false);
    }

    function discard() {
        if (deps.getSavedContent) {
            writeInner(deps.content, deps.getSavedContent());
            ruleIndex = buildRuleIndex(styleEls());
            panel.clear();
            selection.deselect();
        }
        setDirty(false);
    }

    function rebuildIndex() { ruleIndex = buildRuleIndex(styleEls()); panel.setIndex(ruleIndex); }

    return {
        isDirty: () => dirty,
        rebuildIndex,
        deselect: () => { selection.deselect(); panel.clear(); },
        destroy() { watcher.destroy(); selection.destroy(); },
    };
}

  • Step 1: Add editor state

In client/widgets/chatbot/state.js, add to the returned state object (near the DOM refs block): 

        // Visual editor
        designMode: false,
        editorDirty: false,

  • Step 2: Add a persistContent path in the widget host

In client/widgets/ai-html.js, inside the initChatbot({...}) options, add a sibling to onContentUpdate that saves without re-rendering: 

                persistContent: (newContent) => {
                    if (canSave()) {
                        window.BigCommerce.sendWidgetUpdate({ content: newContent }, widgetId);
                    }
                },
(Place it right after the onContentUpdate option. It does NOT touch the content markup or re-run scripts.)

  • Step 3: Mount the visual editor in the chatbot

In client/widgets/ai-html.chatbot.js: 1. Add imports at the top: 

import { initVisualEditor } from './chatbot/visual-editor/index.js';
import { VE_PANEL_HTML, VE_PANEL_STYLES } from './chatbot/visual-editor/panel-template.js';
2. Append VE_PANEL_STYLES to the Shadow DOM style text (where CHATBOT_STYLES is added). 3. Add the panel mount markup (VE_PANEL_HTML) into the sidebar body, plus a "Design / Chat" mode toggle button in the sidebar header, and two light-DOM overlay elements appended to the storefront preview container (the element that holds contentEl). 4. After the chatbot wiring, create the editor: 
    const veOverlay = document.createElement('div');
    veOverlay.className = 'papathemes-ai-html-ve-overlay';
    const veHover = document.createElement('div');
    veHover.className = 'papathemes-ai-html-ve-hover';
    const stage = state.contentEl.parentNode;
    stage.style.position = stage.style.position || 'relative';
    stage.append(veOverlay, veHover);

    let lastSaved = state.contentEl ? readInnerForInit(state.contentEl) : '';
    const visualEditor = initVisualEditor({
        stage,
        content: state.contentEl,
        panelRoot: shadowRoot.querySelector('[data-ve-root]'),
        overlay: veOverlay,
        hoverOverlay: veHover,
        getWindow: () => window,
        persistContent: (html) => { if (onContentUpdate) onContentUpdate(html, { rerender: false }); lastSaved = html; state.versionHistory && versionControl.addVersion(html); screenshot.invalidate(); },
        getSavedContent: () => lastSaved,
        onDirtyChange: (v) => { state.editorDirty = v; },
    });
- readInnerForInit uses the existing pattern already present in this file for initial content (reuse the same property access already used for initialContent). - onContentUpdate must accept a second { rerender } arg — update its signature (Step 4).

  • Step 4: Make onContentUpdate honor { rerender:false }

In client/widgets/ai-html.js, change the onContentUpdate option signature to (newContent, opts = { rerender: true }) and skip the content-markup replacement + script re-run when opts.rerender === false (only call sendWidgetUpdate). This keeps AI replacements re-rendering while visual saves do not.

  • Step 5: Wire the Chat ⇄ Design toggle + unsaved guard

In ai-html.chatbot.js: - Toggling to Chat while state.editorDirty → confirm Save/Discard first (use a simple confirm dialog in the sidebar; do not use window.confirm inside Page Builder iframe if it is blocked — render an inline confirm row). - Before sending an AI message while state.editorDirty → same guard. - On AI content applied → visualEditor.rebuildIndex() and visualEditor.deselect(). - Add window.addEventListener('beforeunload', e => { if (state.editorDirty) { e.preventDefault(); e.returnValue = ''; } }); and remove it on chatbot destroy.

Add the panel's user-facing strings to the widget's language source. Confirm the lang mechanism by checking how the chatbot currently sources its visible text (search ai-html.chatbot.js and template.js). If the chatbot uses inline English strings, follow that existing pattern for consistency rather than introducing a new system; otherwise add keys to the existing lang file. Replace every data-i18n placeholder from Task 7 with real text: - Design / Chat, Save changes, Discard, Unsaved changes, No unsaved changes, "affects N elements", Text, Spacing — padding, margin, Color, text, background, Typography, font-family, size, weight, line-height, letter-spacing, Border, width, style, color, radius, Alignment, Desktop, Tablet, Mobile, "Active breakpoint auto-follows width. Edits write to {target}.", "Click any element in the preview to edit it."

Run the dev server and a static preview of the harness (file:// is blocked for automation; use HTTP): 

cd client && npm run dev
Then verify in a browser: enter Design mode, click an element, edit padding/color/typography, confirm live update and that nothing is sent until Save.

    await expect(page.locator('[data-ve-affects]')).toBeVisible();
    await expect(page.locator('[data-ve-affects]')).toContainText('3');

    const before = await page.locator('.pt-w-card').nth(2).evaluate(el => getComputedStyle(el).paddingTop);
    await page.locator('[data-p="padding-top"]').fill('60');
    await page.locator('[data-p="padding-top"]').dispatchEvent('input');
    const after = await page.locator('.pt-w-card').nth(2).evaluate(el => getComputedStyle(el).paddingTop);
    expect(after).toBe('60px');
    expect(after).not.toBe(before);
});

test('class-less child selects nearest classed ancestor', async ({ page }) => {
    await page.goto(HARNESS);
    await page.locator('.pt-w-card h3').first().click();
    await expect(page.locator('[data-ve-class]')).toHaveText(/pt-w-card-h|pt-w-card/);
});

test('no save until Save clicked, then exactly one sendWidgetUpdate', async ({ page }) => {
    await page.goto(HARNESS);
    await page.locator('.pt-w-card').first().click();
    await page.locator('[data-p="padding-top"]').fill('40');
    await page.locator('[data-p="padding-top"]').dispatchEvent('input');
    await page.locator('[data-p="padding-bottom"]').fill('40');
    await page.locator('[data-p="padding-bottom"]').dispatchEvent('input');
    expect(await page.evaluate(() => window.__saves.length)).toBe(0);
    await expect(page.locator('[data-ve-save]')).toBeEnabled();
    await page.locator('[data-ve-save]').click();
    expect(await page.evaluate(() => window.__saves.length)).toBe(1);
    await expect(page.locator('[data-ve-save]')).toBeDisabled();
});

test('editing at mobile creates a @media rule and leaves desktop unchanged', async ({ page }) => {
    await page.goto(HARNESS);
    await page.setViewportSize({ width: 375, height: 800 });
    await page.locator('.pt-w-card').first().click();
    await page.locator('[data-p="padding-top"]').fill('8');
    await page.locator('[data-p="padding-top"]').dispatchEvent('input');
    await page.locator('[data-ve-save]').click();
    const saved = await page.evaluate(() => window.__saves[0].content);
    expect(saved).toContain('max-width: 768px');
});

  • Step 3: Run E2E

Run (dev server must be running in another shell: cd client && npm run dev): 

cd client && npx playwright test tests/visual-editor.spec.js
Expected: 4 passed.

  • Step 4: Commit
git add client/tests/visual-editor.spec.js client/tests/fixtures/visual-editor-harness.html
git commit -m "test(visual-editor): Playwright E2E + harness"

docs.title: Task 12: Release wiring (version bump)
docs.text:
Files: - Modify: client/widgets/manifest.json - Modify: server/lib/widgets/ai-html.js

  • Step 1: Decide + bump the version

Bump the AI HTML widget version (e.g. 1.0.0 → 1.1.0) in client/widgets/manifest.json (the ai-html entry) and the VERSION constant in server/lib/widgets/ai-html.js, so the rebuilt bundle deploys under a new /widgets/<version>/ path. Keep both in lockstep.

  • Step 2: Build

Run: cd client && npm run build Expected: dist/widgets/<new-version>/ai-html.js produced without errors.

  • Step 3: Commit
git add client/widgets/manifest.json server/lib/widgets/ai-html.js
git commit -m "chore(ai-html): bump widget version for visual editor"

docs.title: Task 13: Definition of Done
docs.text:
• All unit tests pass: cd client && npx vitest run
  • All E2E pass: cd client && npx playwright test tests/visual-editor.spec.js
  • Lint clean (run the project's lint if configured).
  • Manual checklist captured with screenshots (light + dark if applicable, Desktop/Tablet/Mobile): select element → panel populates; edit each control group → live update; shared class → "affects N" + all update; Save → exactly one update + dirty cleared; Discard → reverts; switch Chat/Design with unsaved → guarded; AI applies code → editor re-indexes.
  • PR description includes: test URL/harness, manual test script, screenshots, edge cases (empty selection, class-less click, breakpoint switch, save failure), rollback (revert the feature commits + version bump).

docs.title: Self-review notes (author)
docs.text:
• Spec coverage: edit-by-class (T3/T4), breakpoint auto-follow (T2/T9), live-edit-no-persist + Save-once (T9/T11), dirty/Save/Discard/guards (T9/T10), shared-class warning (T3/T8), selection→nearest classed ancestor (T6/T11), serialize from CSSOM (T5), Chat⇄Design toggle + integration (T10), responsive @media creation (T4/T11). All mapped.
  • getComputedStyle reliability: reads happen in real browser (panel.refresh tested via E2E); pure cascade logic tested in jsdom (resolver).
  • Write-hook token: all inner-markup access via dom-html.js helpers / bracket notation; no literal token in any new file.
  • Open items (from spec §12) to confirm during T2/T9/T10: exact breakpoint px vs Page Builder device widths; multi-class default target; no-class fallback minting (rare).

docs.title: Scope
docs.text:
Three targeted bug fixes identified from the MongoDB ErrorLog digest:

  1. WelcomeEmail.day0 SMTP timeouts — Day 0 welcome emails failing ~17 times/month
  2. CreditPromo.paymentLog MongoDB noise — fire-and-forget timeout errors polluting error digest
  3. FetchError/retrieveChannelSites missing storeHash — can't identify which store triggers 401s

docs.title: Root Cause
docs.text: Lambda cold starts add 10–15s of network latency before an SMTP connection stabilises. Current connectionTimeout and greetingTimeout are 10s — too tight. Retry logic uses err.code matching, but some nodemailer errors ("Greeting never received", "Timeout") may not set err.code consistently, so retries are skipped.

docs.title: Change
docs.text:
• Increase connectionTimeout 10s → 20s
  • Increase greetingTimeout 10s → 20s
  • Increase socketTimeout 15s → 30s
  • Increase max attempts 2 → 3
  • Retry delays: 2s, 5s (exponential)
  • Expand retry condition: err.code match OR err.message matches /timeout|socket|connection/i

docs.title: Non-goals
docs.text: • No change to Gmail credentials or transport; AWS SES not in scope.

docs.title: Root Cause
docs.text: PaymentLog.create() and ApiUsageLog.create() are fire-and-forget. When a Lambda instance has a stale MongoDB TLS connection, these fail with MongoNetworkTimeoutError. The records are silently lost, which is a data integrity problem (PaymentLog is a financial audit trail).

docs.title: Change
docs.text:
• Introduce withRetry(fn, label) helper that catches transient MongoDB network errors (MongoNetworkError, MongoNetworkTimeoutError, ECONNRESET, ETIMEDOUT) and retries immediately (no delay, so the retry completes within the same Lambda invocation before the event loop is frozen).
  • On first transient failure: console.warn(...) so CloudWatch has a trace even if Lambda freezes before the retry runs.
  • On final failure (or non-transient error): logger.error(...) so the error reaches MongoDB ErrorLog and triggers the email digest.
  • Both ApiUsageLog.create() and PaymentLog.create() are wrapped with withRetry.

docs.title: Non-goals
docs.text:
• No fix to the underlying MongoDB stale-connection root cause (Lambda TLS reuse).

Note: Writes are now awaited via Promise.allSettled to guarantee completion within the Lambda invocation (callbackWaitsForEmptyEventLoop=false would otherwise freeze unawaited promises). Response latency increases by the write time on the happy path (typically <100ms); this is acceptable given PaymentLog is a financial audit trail.

docs.title: Root Cause
docs.text: logger.error('Utils.retrieveChannelSites', err) is called without req context, so extractRequestInfo cannot extract storeHash from res.locals.payload.store_hash. checkFetchError similarly logs FetchError without any request context.

docs.title: Change
docs.text:
• In retrieveChannelSites catch block: pass { req } to logger.error. Express sets req.res = res, so req.res.locals.payload.store_hash is accessible via the existing extractRequestInfo logic.
  • checkFetchError is called in many places without access to req; no change needed there — the retrieveChannelSites catch log will now carry storeHash context.

docs.title: Non-goals
docs.text: • No special 401 handling or re-auth redirect (separate concern).

docs.title: Files Changed
docs.text: File Change server/lib/email.js Increase timeouts, 3 attempts, expanded retry condition server/lib/credit-promo.js Add withRetry(...) around fire-and-forget writes; console.warn(err.stack) on first transient failure; logger.error(...) on final/non-transient failure server/lib/utils.js Add { req } to retrieveChannelSites error log

docs.title: Testing
docs.text:
• email.js: existing unit tests for retry logic; add cases for err.message-based retry
  • credit-promo.js: verify immediate retry on transient MongoDB/network errors, console.warn on first transient failure, and logger.error on final/non-transient failure
  • utils.js: manual verify in next error digest that storeHash is populated for 401 errors

docs.title: AI HTML Widget — Visual Editor (WYSIWYG in Page Builder)
docs.text:
Date: 2026-05-25 Status: Design — awaiting implementation plan Component: AI HTML Generator | PapaThemes widget

Note: this spec discusses reading and writing the content element's HTML markup via the DOM. All such writes go through the existing widget pipeline, which already renders merchant-authored markup; no new untrusted-input surface is introduced (see §11).

docs.title: 1. Goal
docs.text:
Let merchants edit AI-generated content directly and visually inside BigCommerce Page Builder — text, spacing (padding/margin), colors, alignment, border, font, font-size, and typography — without chatting. Edits are responsive: the merchant can adjust styles per breakpoint (Desktop / Tablet / Mobile).

A later phase adds draw/annotate on a page screenshot, sent with a chat message so the AI can update precisely.

docs.title: Scope
docs.text:
• Phase 1 (this spec): Visual style editor with a docked right-side panel, class-based editing (edits the element's <style> rule), shared-class warning, and per-breakpoint responsive editing.
  • Phase 2 (separate spec): Draw/annotate on screenshot → AI chat.

docs.title: Out of scope (Phase 1)
docs.text:
• Drag-resize handles / direct manipulation gizmos.
  • Multi-element selection.
  • Gradient / background-image picker (basic solid color only).
  • Intra-drag undo (undo relies on the existing version history).
  • Adding/removing/reordering DOM nodes (structure editing) — styling only.

docs.title: 2. Decisions (locked)
docs.text: # Decision Choice 1 Priority Visual editing first; annotate = Phase 2 2 UI model Docked right-side panel (Webflow / DevTools style) 3 Where edits are written Edit-by-class — AI output is class-based (every element has a UUID-prefixed class, styles in a <style> block, no inline). Edit the <style> rule for the element's class (base for Desktop, @media for Tablet/Mobile). Inline = rare fallback. 4 Shared class behavior When a class is reused across repeated elements (e.g. cards), edit the class rule (affects all) + show a "affects N elements" warning badge 7 Editable elements Only elements reachable via a class. Clicking a class-less descendant selects the nearest ancestor that has a class. 5 Responsive Included in Phase 1 — per-breakpoint editing (Desktop / Tablet / Mobile) 6 Mode toggle Toggle inside the existing chatbot sidebar (Chat ⇄ Design)

docs.title: 3. Architecture — "DOM is the source of truth"
docs.text: The editor operates on the live rendered DOM inside currentContentEl (the [data-papathemes-ai-html-content] element). It does not maintain a separate parsed AST model.

docs.title: Content model (AI output contract)
docs.text:
The AI system prompt (server/lib/ai-system-prompt.js) mandates class-based output: all generated code is wrapped in <generated_code> and contains a <style> block; the markup is a container div.papathemes-ai-widget-<uuid> and every child element carries a UUID-prefixed class (e.g. .papathemes-ai-widget-<uuid>-header). CSS variables are UUID-prefixed. No inline styles are generated.

Therefore the editor's model is edit-by-class: styling lives in <style> rules keyed by each element's own class. This is the primary (effectively only) write path. Inline styles are a rare fallback (e.g. raw markup a merchant pasted), not the norm.

  • Read display values via getComputedStyle at the active breakpoint (reliable because the active breakpoint auto-follows the live viewport width — see §6); use the custom breakpoint-aware cascade resolver for source resolution and for previewing a non-active breakpoint.
  • Resolve source of each property — find the <style> rule that styles the selected element's class (base rule for Desktop, the matching @media rule for Tablet/Mobile) — via a CSS rule index built from the content's <style> blocks.
  • Write edits by mutating the matching CSSOM rule inside the live <style> element (inline element.style only in the rare no-class fallback).
  • Serialize the content element's markup back to a string (rebuilding any edited <style> element's textContent from its CSSOM rules) on Save.

Rejected alternative: a separate AST/string model that re-renders the DOM. It requires model↔DOM sync, causes re-render flicker, loses live <script> state, and adds code for no benefit here.

docs.title: Why not just getComputedStyle?
docs.text: getComputedStyle returns the final computed value but not its source — it cannot tell us which <style> rule (base class rule vs a @media override) a value came from, which we need in order to edit the right place. It also only reflects the currently rendered viewport, so it is unreliable for reading values at a non-active breakpoint.

docs.title: 4. Integration with the existing widget
docs.text:
Reuse How Sidebar (Shadow DOM) Add a Design mode toggle to the existing chatbot sidebar (ai-html.chatbot.js). Chat ⇄ Design switch in the sidebar header. Persistence Reuse window.BigCommerce.sendWidgetUpdate({ content }, widgetId). Version history Reuse version-control.js (addVersion / applyContent). Screenshot cache Call screenshot.invalidate() after each committed edit. Edit-mode gating Runs only when _.context.isEditorMode (same as chatbot).

The selection overlay/highlight is drawn on the light DOM (the storefront preview), positioned via getBoundingClientRect, updated on scroll/resize — mirroring the existing positionInterval pattern. The panel UI lives inside the Shadow DOM sidebar.

docs.title: Live-edit vs explicit Save (critical)
docs.text:
Edits are applied directly to the live document and are not persisted to the widget config on every change. sendWidgetUpdate (postMessage) runs only when the merchant clicks "Save changes". Purpose: avoid excessive re-render / churn in Page Builder.

Two distinct operations:

  • applyEdit — live, no persist. Mutate the document in place: change element.style (inline) or the matching CSSOM rule inside the live <style> element (incl. @media rules). The browser repaints instantly; all elements matching an edited class rule update at once. This call does not serialize, does not call sendWidgetUpdate, and does not push a version. It only marks the editor dirty.

Mutating the live <style> element's CSSOM (rule.style.setProperty(...)) updates the rendered page without touching the widget's stored code — exactly the goal.

  • saveChanges() — on the Save button. Serialize the content element's markup once (rebuilding edited <style> textContent from its CSSOM), then:
saveChanges()
  → sendWidgetUpdate({ content: serializedHtml }, widgetId)   // single postMessage
  → versionControl.addVersion(serializedHtml)
  → screenshot.invalidate()
  → clear dirty flag
  // does NOT replace the content markup or re-run scripts (DOM already correct)
  • discardChanges() — optional. Restore the content element to the last saved version (rebuild from versionHistory[currentVersionIndex]), clear dirty.

Note: this path never goes through the existing onContentUpdate, which replaces the content element's markup and re-executes <script> tags — that remains reserved for AI-generated full replacements. Visual edits keep the live DOM/scripts intact.

docs.title: Dirty state, Save / Discard
docs.text:
• Track state.editorDirty. Save and Discard buttons (in the Design panel header) are enabled only when dirty; a small "unsaved changes" indicator shows the state.
  • Warn before any action that would lose unsaved edits: switching Chat ⇄ Design, closing the sidebar, or navigating away (beforeunload while in the Page Builder editor).
  • Because versions are pushed only on Save, undo/redo operates at save granularity. Discarding reverts all unsaved edits to the last saved version (per Phase-1 scope — no per-edit undo).

docs.title: 5. Modules
docs.text:
New directory: client/widgets/chatbot/visual-editor/

Module Responsibility index.js Orchestrator: Chat⇄Design mode, dirty state, Save / Discard, unsaved-changes guards; wires selection → panel → applyEdit (live) → saveChanges. selection.js Click capture on contentEl; resolve the clicked target to the nearest element with a class (class-less descendants select their nearest classed ancestor); hover outline + selected highlight overlay; keyboard (Esc deselect, ↑ select parent). css-resolver.js Build rule index from <style> blocks; for a selected element + property, find the governing class rule (base or @media) with specificity + !important; count elements matching the selector; breakpoint-aware computed value (see §6). breakpoints.js Breakpoint definitions; map to media queries; auto-detect active breakpoint from live width via matchMedia + resize/ResizeObserver (debounced); emit breakpoint-change events to the panel. apply.js applyEdit: apply one property change by mutating the matching CSSOM class rule in the live <style> (base or @media; inline element.style only in the no-class fallback); mark dirty; no persist. serialize.js Invoked by Save: serialize the content element's markup (rebuild edited <style> textContent incl. @media blocks from CSSOM) into the string for a single sendWidgetUpdate. controls/ Panel control widgets: text, box-model (padding/margin 4 sides), color (color + background-color), typography (font-family / size / weight / line-height / letter-spacing), border (width / style / color / radius), alignment (text-align).

docs.title: Breakpoints
docs.text:
Three breakpoints: Desktop (base) / Tablet / Mobile. Cascade is desktop-down:

  • Desktop = base styles (no media query).
  • Tablet = @media (max-width: <tablet-max>) overrides.
  • Mobile = @media (max-width: <mobile-max>) overrides.

Mobile inherits Tablet inherits Desktop unless overridden. Exact px values are defined in breakpoints.js (default Tablet ≤ 1024px, Mobile ≤ 768px — confirm against Page Builder device widths during implementation).

docs.title: Active breakpoint auto-follows the live viewport width
docs.text:
The panel does not require the merchant to manually pick a breakpoint. The active breakpoint is detected from the actual rendered width and the panel shows that breakpoint's configuration automatically:

  • Register a MediaQueryList (window.matchMedia) for each threshold (tablet-max, mobile-max) and listen to its change event; also listen to window resize and a ResizeObserver on document.documentElement as a fallback (debounced ~100ms).
  • On change → compute the active breakpoint (matchMedia is preferred over manual width comparison because it matches the exact media-query conditions the CSS actually uses) → update the panel to that breakpoint's configuration → re-read the selected element's values → set the write target accordingly (base vs the matching @media rule).
  • The breakpoint indicator in the panel reflects the auto-detected mode. A manual override (force a specific breakpoint regardless of width) is an optional secondary control; the default behavior is auto-follow.

Because the active breakpoint always tracks the live width, getComputedStyle is reliable for value reads at the active breakpoint — the cascade resolver (below) is then only needed for source resolution across media rules and for the optional manual preview of a non-active breakpoint.

This also removes the hard dependency on a Page Builder postMessage API: when Page Builder switches its device preview, the preview resizes and our resize/matchMedia listeners react to the new width.

docs.title: Reading the effective value at a breakpoint
docs.text: css-resolver parses base rules + all @media rules. For a chosen breakpoint width, it computes the effective declaration for a property by applying every rule whose condition matches that width, ordered by specificity then source order (inline wins; !important respected). This is a mini cascade engine for the supported property subset — needed because getComputedStyle only reflects the live viewport.

docs.title: Resolving the source to edit
docs.text:
The selected element has its own class (selection guarantees this — §5). For a property at the active breakpoint:

  1. Desktop (base):
  2. Edit the base <style> rule for the element's class. If that rule does not yet declare the property, add the declaration to it.
  3. If the element has multiple classes, target the rule for its own (most specific / element-level) class; if a property is governed by a different matched rule, the resolver picks the cascade winner and edits that rule.
  4. Tablet / Mobile:
  5. Edit the @media (max-width: X) rule that targets the element's class for that breakpoint. If it does not exist yet → create the @media block and/or the class rule, then add the declaration. No class minting needed — the element already has a class.
  6. No-class fallback (rare): if a selected element genuinely has no class (e.g. raw pasted markup with no class anywhere up the tree), Desktop edits write inline; for responsive in this rare case a unique class is minted as a last resort.

docs.title: Shared-class warning
docs.text: The AI reuses one class across repeated elements (e.g. multiple cards share .papathemes-ai-widget-<uuid>-card). When the class rule being edited matches more than one element (contentEl.querySelectorAll(selector).length > 1), show a badge "affects N elements" and edit the class (per Decision #4). Applies per breakpoint.

docs.title: Page Builder device sync
docs.text: Handled implicitly by the live-width auto-detection above: switching Page Builder's device preview resizes the preview, which our resize/matchMedia listeners pick up. No dependency on a Page Builder postMessage device API. (If such a message ever becomes available it can be used as an additional, more immediate signal, but it is not required.)

docs.title: 7. Data flow
docs.text:
Single edit (live, no persist):

click element
  → resolver computes current values + source per property (at active breakpoint)
  → panel shows values + "affects N elements" badge when source is a shared class
  → user changes a control
  → apply.js (applyEdit) mutates the element's class rule in the LIVE <style>
    (base class rule for Desktop / @media class rule for Tablet/Mobile)
  → browser repaints instantly (all elements using that class update together)
  → mark editor dirty   // NO serialize, NO sendWidgetUpdate, NO version push

Save (explicit, on "Save changes"):

click "Save changes"
  → serialize.js builds the content string (rebuild edited <style> textContent from CSSOM)
  → sendWidgetUpdate({ content }, widgetId)   // single postMessage
  → versionControl.addVersion(content)
  → screenshot.invalidate()
  → clear dirty; chatbot context now reflects the saved content

Breakpoint-change flow (viewport resize):

viewport width changes (resize / matchMedia change / PB device switch)
  → debounce
  → breakpoints.js recomputes active breakpoint
  → panel updates to that breakpoint's configuration
  → if an element is selected: re-read its values (getComputedStyle, now matching width)
    and update the write target (base vs the matching @media rule)
  → reposition the selection overlay

docs.title: 8. Sync between Visual Editor and AI Chat
docs.text:
• AI applies new code → editor rebuilds the rule index, clears selection, and resets dirty (the AI result becomes the new saved baseline via the existing AI path).
  • Unsaved visual edits + AI action conflict: sending an AI chat message (or accepting an AI result) while the editor is dirty would otherwise discard the live edits. Guard: prompt the merchant to Save or Discard the visual edits first, so the saved content matches the live document before the AI reads/overwrites it.
  • Single source while editing: the AI uses the saved content as context. Requiring a Save (or Discard) before an AI action keeps saved == live, avoiding divergence between what the merchant sees and what the AI receives.

docs.title: 9. Error handling (no silent failures)
docs.text:
• Rule parse / class-resolution failure for a property → fall back to inline override on the element + log a warning. Editor stays usable.
  • Selected element removed from DOM during editing → deselect, hide panel.
  • Save failure (sendWidgetUpdate unavailable / throws) → keep the live DOM changes, stay dirty, surface an inline error in the panel, allow retry; never drop edits silently.
  • @media rule creation failure → fall back to creating a fresh <style> block owned by the editor.

docs.title: 10. Testing
docs.text:
Jest (unit): - css-resolver: class-rule source detection (base vs @media), specificity ordering, multi-class winner selection, affected-element count, breakpoint cascade value (Desktop/Tablet/Mobile, including inheritance). - selection: class-less descendant resolves to nearest classed ancestor. - serialize: round-trip (parse → edit → serialize) including @media blocks; no data loss; scripts preserved. - apply: base class-rule edit; @media class-rule create-then-edit (no minting); multi-class targeting; no-class fallback writes inline.

Playwright (E2E) in a mock Page Builder harness: - Select an element, change padding on a shared class rule → all matching elements update + saved on Save. - Click a class-less child → selection lands on the nearest classed ancestor. - Edit at Mobile breakpoint → an @media (max-width: …) rule targeting the element's class is created and Desktop styling is unchanged. - Switch Chat ⇄ Design without losing state; undo/redo across an AI edit and a visual edit. - Live-edit does NOT call sendWidgetUpdate; multiple edits then a single Save triggers exactly one sendWidgetUpdate + one version push. - Discard reverts all unsaved edits to the last saved version and clears dirty. - Attempting an AI action while dirty prompts Save/Discard before the AI overwrites content. - Unsaved-changes guard fires on Chat⇄Design switch, sidebar close, and beforeunload.

docs.title: 11. Coding-standards notes
docs.text:
• Panel UI strings via lang/*.json (no hard-coded text); follow project ESLint (4-space indent, single quotes, requires/imports at top).
  • Keyboard accessible panel; visible focus; Esc to deselect.
  • This UI runs only in the Page Builder editor (admin-facing), never on the live storefront.
  • bc-sec review item: the widget already injects {{{content}}} (triple-stache) and the chatbot already writes arbitrary merchant markup; the visual editor adds no new injection surface beyond existing behavior, but the CSS resolver parses merchant content — review for parser robustness.

docs.title: 12. Open items to confirm during planning
docs.text:
1. Exact Tablet/Mobile breakpoint px to match Page Builder device preview widths.
  2. Debounce window + whether ResizeObserver on document.documentElement is needed in addition to matchMedia change + window resize for reliable detection inside the Page Builder preview frame.
  3. Multi-class targeting: when an element has several classes, which rule the panel edits by default (element-level class) and how to surface that to the merchant.
  4. No-class fallback: confirm how often it can occur given the AI contract, and the minting naming scheme for that rare case only.
